entries.name: Scotch-Weld CA5
entries.summary: Slow-setting higher-viscosity instant adhesive for rough or uneven parts where you want a little alignment time before lockup.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M Scotch-Weld Instant Adhesive CA5 Technical Data Sheet, last revision December 2024, supersedes June 2024; PDF creation date December 11, 2024
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
entries.serviceTemperatureNote: TDS prints Long Term Temperature Resistance -82 C (180 F) and Minimum Long Term Temperature Resistance -54 C (-65 F); 180 F corresponds to +82 C, so the printed -82 C appears to be a sign error.
entries.potLifeConditions: Master Bond product page states unlimited working life at room temperature for the one-component system.
entries.fixtureTimeNote: Published as Time to Handling Strength 12-60 s.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Not published in cached official 3M CA5 TDS; previous 17.2 MPa value is intentionally removed as unsupported by this source.
cautions: Pot life is not applicable/not published for this one-component instant adhesive.
Thermal conductivity and lap-shear strength are not published in the cached official 3M TDS.
For optimum strength structural bonds, remove paint, oxide films, oils, dust, mold release agents and other contaminants.
When using solvents, extinguish ignition sources and follow solvent manufacturer precautions.
Store refrigerated at 0-4 C / 32-40 F; do not freeze; keep containers tightly covered and free of moisture; avoid direct sun.
Industrial/occupational product; not designed or tested for certain automotive applications including electric powertrain battery or high-voltage applications.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Thick liquid (2,000 cP)
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite, metal, plastic, rubber
entries.priceExample: 1 oz bottle ~$46.60

Fills gaps up to 0.020 in / 0.508 mm and is intended for non-sag vertical applications where repositioning time is useful.
For optimum strength structural bonds, remove paint, oxide films, oils, dust, mold release agents and other contaminants.
When using solvents, extinguish ignition sources and follow solvent manufacturer precautions.
Store refrigerated at 0-4 C / 32-40 F; do not freeze; keep containers tightly covered and free of moisture; avoid direct sun.
Industrial/occupational product; not designed or tested for certain automotive applications including electric powertrain battery or high-voltage applications.

entries.name: Scotch-Weld PR1500
entries.summary: High-viscosity plastic-and-rubber instant adhesive with good gap filling and less unwanted wicking into nearby seams.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M Scotch-Weld Plastic and Rubber Instant Adhesives Technical Data, February 2016; PDF creation date June 21, 2016
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl hybrid cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
entries.fixtureTimeNote: Published as Time to Handling Strength on EPDM; substrate dependent.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Steel, ASTM D1002, grit blasted: 2416 psi / 16.66 MPa.
entries.electronicsCompletenessReason: Official Permabond TDS positions this as a general instant adhesive; dielectric values are material properties, not an electronics adhesive application claim.
cautions: Pot life is not applicable/not published for this one-component instant adhesive.
Thermal conductivity is not published in the cached official 3M TDS.
Activator may be required for bonding gaps, porous surfaces, low-surface-energy plastics such as polyethylene/polypropylene, or acidic surfaces such as paper/leather.
Align parts before dispensing because bond speed is typically very fast.
Apply sparingly; over-application slows cure and lowers bond strength.
Long-term humidity, moisture, or water immersion may affect cured bond strength depending on substrate and bond gap; testing is recommended.
When using solvents, extinguish ignition sources and follow solvent manufacturer precautions.
Short-term storage below 30 days: 16-27 C / 60-80 F dry place out of direct sunlight; refrigeration at 4 C / 40 F gives optimum long-term stability.

entries.name: Scotch-Weld DP8810NS
entries.summary: Low-odor non-sag structural acrylic for fast strength build on metal, plastic, composite, and powder-coated assemblies.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M Scotch-Weld Low Odor Acrylic Adhesive DP8810NS Green Technical Data Sheet, last revision February 2025, supersedes September 2024
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 B:A mix by volume or weight. Mixed worklife is 8-12 minutes at 23 C, open time is 10 minutes, set time is 16-20 minutes to 0.3 MPa overlap shear, time to structural strength is 19-23 minutes to 6.9 MPa overlap shear, and full cure is 24 h to at least 80% of ultimate aluminum-aluminum overlap-shear strength.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Aluminum overlap shear after 7 d at 23 C, light abrasion and solvent clean, ASTM D1002 / ISO 4587: 23.4 MPa / 3400 psi cohesive failure.
entries.peelStrength: Bell peel on etched aluminum, ASTM D3167: 61 N/cm / 35 lb/in cohesive failure after 24 h room-temperature cure.
entries.viscosityUnit: cP mixed; component viscosities measured at 23 C and 3.8 sec^-1 shear rate by cone-and-plate viscometer
entries.ulRecognized: UL File QOQW2.MH17478 and Sign Components Manual File E464624 for electrical equipment/sign components
cautions: The TDS values are representative or typical only and should not be used for specification purposes.
Environmental aging tests show these adhesives may accelerate corrosion of certain bare metals such as cold-rolled steel, copper, brass and bronze, leading to low bond strength and early failure.
Adhesion to low surface energy plastics such as polypropylene, polyethylene, TPO and PTFE is relatively low; evaluate suitability carefully.
Oxygen inhibits cure of exposed mixed adhesive surfaces; exposed low-odor acrylic may remain as a wet partially cured film.
Allow adhesive to cure at 16 C / 60 F or above; heat up to 66 C / 150 F increases cure speed.
For glass bonding, 3M recommends acetone cleaning and a thin silane adhesion-promoter coating.
Industrial/occupational product; not for consumer sale or use.
The TDS does not publish continuous service-temperature limits or thermal conductivity.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Blue-green mixed non-sag toughened acrylic paste; mixed viscosity 45,000 cP; B:A 10:1 mix ratio; 0.010 in glass beads for bond-line control
entries.referenceForJoining: Structural bonding of aluminum, stainless steel, PVC, ABS, acrylic, polycarbonate, FRP, fiber-reinforced epoxy, painted/coated metals, plastics, composites and powder-coated surfaces

entries.name: VHB 4950
entries.summary: White 1.1 mm double-sided general-purpose acrylic foam VHB tape with exact 3M TDS data: 44 N/cm 90-degree peel on stainless steel, 970 kPa normal tensile on aluminum, 550 kPa overlap shear on stainless steel, 93 C long-term and 149 C short-term temperature resistance, immediate handling strength and 24-month shelf life.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M VHB Tape - Specialty Tape 4950 Technical Data Sheet, last revision September 2024
entries.chemistry: General-purpose acrylic pressure-sensitive adhesive on firm acrylic foam
entries.cureDetail: Pressure-sensitive acrylic foam tape; no mix or liquid cure cycle. Bond strength builds by adhesive wet-out: about 50% ultimate after 20 min, 90% after 24 h and 100% after 72 h at room temperature.
entries.minimumApplicationTemperatureC: 10-15 C suggested minimum for most 3M VHB tapes; ideal application range 21-38 C
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Overlap shear strength on stainless steel, ASTM D1002 / ISO 4587, 72 h dwell at 23 C, 6.45 cm2 area, jaw speed 12.7 mm/min.
cautions: The 3M TDS gives maximum long-term and short-term temperature resistance but does not publish a lower service-temperature limit, so serviceMin is intentionally omitted.
This is a pressure-sensitive foam tape, not a liquid adhesive; pot life and viscosity are not liquid properties.

Most substrates should be cleaned with a 70/30 IPA/water mixture before application; heavy oils, oxidized surfaces, porous materials, glass-like materials, copper-containing metals, and plasticizer-containing plastics or rubbers may need additional preparation.
Initial application below the suggested minimum temperature is not recommended because the adhesive becomes too firm to adhere readily.
Use enough tape area for expected loads; for static loads 3M gives about 57 cm2 of tape per kg of supported weight to prevent excessive creep.
VHB tapes are viscoelastic: stronger under faster dynamic loading and prone to creep under long-duration static stress.
For rigid substrates with normal flatness, 3M recommends tape thickness of 45 mil / 1.1 mm or greater; VHB conforms to some irregularity but will not flow to fill large gaps.
Industrial product not designed or tested for certain automotive electric powertrain battery or high-voltage applications.
entries.referenceSampleType: Roll of white 0.045 in / 1.1 mm double-sided acrylic foam tape with white printed DK paper liner
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: White firm acrylic foam pressure-sensitive tape; 800 kg/m3 density; 1.1 mm total thickness; 0.08 mm liner; 72 h dwell performance data
entries.referenceForJoining: High-surface-energy substrates including stainless steel, aluminum, metals, glass, easier-to-bond paints and plastics, and rigid assemblies needing permanent sealed joints with immediate handling strength

entries.name: VHB 4945
entries.summary: White 1.1 mm double-coated multi-purpose acrylic foam VHB tape with exact 3M TDS data: 44 N/cm 90-degree peel on stainless steel, 970 kPa normal tensile on aluminum, 550 kPa overlap shear, 93 C long-term and 149 C short-term temperature resistance, plasticized-vinyl resistance data, low outgassing values and 24-month shelf life.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M VHB Tape - Specialty Tape 4945 Technical Data Sheet, last revision May 2022
entries.chemistry: Multi-purpose acrylic pressure-sensitive adhesive on firm acrylic foam
entries.cureDetail: Pressure-sensitive acrylic foam tape; no mix or liquid cure cycle. Bond strength builds by adhesive wet-out: about 50% ultimate after 20 min, 90% after 24 h and 100% after 72 h at room temperature.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Overlap shear strength, ASTM D1002, 72 h dwell at 23 C, 6.45 cm2 area, jaw speed 12.7 mm/min.
cautions: The 3M TDS gives maximum long-term and short-term temperature resistance but does not publish a lower service-temperature limit, so serviceMin is intentionally omitted.
This is a pressure-sensitive foam tape, not a liquid adhesive; pot life and viscosity are not liquid properties.

Most substrates are best prepared with a 50/50 IPA/water mixture before application; heavy oils, oxidized surfaces, porous materials, glass-like materials, copper-containing metals, and plasticizer-containing plastics or rubbers may need additional preparation.
Minimum suggested application temperature for the 4945 family is 15 C; application below this is not recommended because the adhesive becomes too firm to adhere readily.
Plasticized vinyl formulations vary; 3M states users must evaluate the exact vinyl and suggests accelerated aging at 66 C for one week to predict plasticizer-migration issues.
Use enough tape area for expected loads; for static loads 3M gives about 57 cm2 of tape per kg of supported weight to prevent excessive creep.
VHB tapes are viscoelastic: stronger under faster dynamic loading and prone to creep under long-duration static stress.
For rigid substrates with normal flatness, 3M recommends tape thickness of 45 mil / 1.1 mm or greater; VHB conforms to some irregularity but will not flow to fill gaps.
Applications requiring severe cold-temperature impact performance must be thoroughly evaluated by the user.
Industrial product not designed or tested for certain automotive electric powertrain battery or high-voltage applications.
entries.referenceSampleType: Roll of white 0.045 in / 1.1 mm double-coated acrylic foam tape with white printed DK paper liner
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: White firm acrylic foam pressure-sensitive tape; 800 kg/m3 density; 1.1 mm total thickness; 0.08 mm liner; 72 h dwell performance data
entries.referenceForJoining: High and medium surface-energy substrates including metals, glass, paints and plastics, plus plasticized vinyl applications where the user validates plasticizer migration over time

entries.summary: Oil-tolerant medium-strength threadlocker for serviceable fasteners that still need vibration resistance and corrosion sealing.

Standard cured properties use 24 h at 23 C.

entries.name: AA 3492
entries.summary: Transparent low-viscosity one-part modified-acrylate UV adhesive with exact Henkel TDS data: 350-600 mPa.s viscosity, 1-20 s glass fixture depending on 365 nm intensity, 4.1 MPa glass-glass lap shear, 8.3 MPa steel-glass block shear, 8.8 N/mm glass peel, Shore D 79 hardness and strong humidity, immersion and dishwasher-cycle retention.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: LOCTITE AA 3492 Technical Data Sheet, November 2014; PDF digitally signed by tdx.henkel.com November 26, 2025
entries.cureDetail: One-part modified acrylate that cures in seconds under 365 nm UV light; fixture time is defined in the TDS as time to 0.1 N/mm2 shear strength on glass microscope slides.
entries.cureIntensityMwCm2: 6, 30 or 100 mW/cm2 fixture data; 40 mW/cm2 minimum recommended at the bondline for adhesive cure; 100 mW/cm2 minimum for tack-free coating, potting or tacking surface cure.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Glass-to-glass lap shear strength, ISO 4587, from Henkel TDS.
cautions: The Henkel TDS does not publish a continuous service-temperature range; heat ageing at 121 C and 149 C is retained as test data rather than encoded as serviceMax.
This product is light sensitive; exposure to daylight, UV light and artificial lighting should be minimized during storage and handling.
Henkel recommends black feedlines for dispensing.
Cure rate depends on lamp intensity, distance from light source, required depth of cure or bondline gap, and UV transmittance of the substrate.
Recommended adhesive-cure intensity is 40 mW/cm2 minimum at the bondline with exposure time 5-6 times the fixture time at the same intensity.
Tack-free surface cure for coating, potting or tacking requires higher intensity UV of at least 100 mW/cm2.
Provide cooling for temperature-sensitive thermoplastics.
Plastic grades should be checked for liquid-adhesive stress-cracking risk.
Excess uncured adhesive can be wiped away with organic solvent such as acetone.
Not recommended for pure oxygen or oxygen-rich systems, or as a sealant for chlorine or other strong oxidizing materials.
Store unopened dry at 8 to 21 C; storage below 8 C or above 28 C can adversely affect product properties.

entries.lapShearSubstrate: Aluminum/alumite tensile shear after 23 C / 50% RH x 7 d; full substrate table retained in shearStrengthsMPa.
cautions: CEMEDINE positions this as a curable thermal interface / adhesive, not as a rigid structural epoxy.
Reference page lists made-to-order packaging for SX1008.
The page does not publish a continuous service-temperature range or pot life for SX1008; heat-aging data is reported separately at 120 C and 130 C on aluminum.
Open time for SX1008 and SX1010 is listed as no open-time in the substrate shear test condition.
CEMEDINE reports low-molecular-silicone-free behavior to reduce electrical contact-failure risk, but users must validate contamination sensitivity in the actual device.

SX720 is siloxane-free with no D3-D10 low-molecular cyclosiloxane, but users should still validate electrical contact compatibility in the finished assembly.

Clean and dry bonding surfaces are essential; WEICON recommends cleaning/degreasing with WEICON Surface Cleaner and mechanical roughening for smooth surfaces.
The first 5 cm of adhesive bead from the static mixer should be discarded.
The adhesive is applied to just one side.
Larger batch quantities cure faster; temperatures below +16 C significantly increase pot life/cure time and from approximately +5 C and below no reaction takes place.
The TDS does not publish thermal conductivity, dielectric properties, chemical-resistance table or detailed shelf-life conditions beyond room/refrigerated minimum shelf life.

entries.sourceRevisionDate: WEICON VA 2407 Cyanoacrylate Adhesive TDS, issued February 18, 2026
entries.cureDetail: 4:1 two-component cyanoacrylate adhesive. Initial adhesion is measured at 23 C / 50% RH to 0.5 MPa shear strength: about 4 s on sandblasted aluminum, 84 s on untreated ABS and 55 s on rigid PVC. Pot life is 5 min at 20 C for a 10 g batch and final strength is reached after 24 h.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: DIN EN 1465 shear strength: steel sandblasted 16-21 MPa, aluminum sandblasted 10-15 MPa, rigid untreated PVC 10-14 MPa, untreated ABS 13-15 MPa and polycarbonate 12-17 MPa.

curingSchedules.context: initial adhesion to 0.5 MPa shear strength
initial adhesion to 0.5 MPa shear strength
initial adhesion to 0.5 MPa shear strength

entries.electronicsCompletenessReason: Official WEICON TDS positions VA 2407 as a general transparent structural/plastic/rubber two-component cyanoacrylate. Published resistivity and dielectric strength are generic material properties, not electronics adhesive application data.
cautions: The TDS specifications and recommendations are not guaranteed product characteristics; users must run adequate lab and practical tests for the application.
Successful application depends on thorough surface preparation.
Use physical, safety, toxicological and ecological data from WEICON EC safety data sheets when applying.
The TDS does not publish detailed chemical-resistance tables despite product-page statements about resistance to many aggressive chemicals.
Scalar lapShear uses the lower bound for steel; full substrate ranges are preserved in shearStrengthsMPa.

Do not count generic dielectric strength or volume resistivity as electronics-performance evidence; the TDS does not position this as a PCB, potting, conformal coating, conductive adhesive, die-attach, or electronics assembly adhesive.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Crystal-clear two-component cyanoacrylate paste; 4:1 mix; 1,000 mPa.s; 1.1 g/cm3 density
entries.referenceForJoining: Polycarbonate, plastics, steel, stainless steel, aluminum, ceramics, glass, rubber

entries.name: Easy-Mix HT 250
entries.summary: High-temperature light-gray two-part epoxy with exact WEICON TDS data: 2:1 mix, 38,000 mPa.s viscosity, 4 mm gap bridging, 150 min pot life, 20 MPa steel lap shear, 1.4 W/m.K thermal conductivity, >24 kV/mm dielectric strength and -50 to 200 C continuous service with brief 250 C exposure.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: WEICON Easy-Mix HT 250 Epoxy Adhesive Technical Data Sheet, issued February 18, 2026
entries.cureDetail: Two-component epoxy resin system. Apply from the 50 ml 2:1 double cartridge through a static mixer after discarding the first 5 cm of bead. Pot life is 150 min for a 10 ml batch at 20 C; 50% working strength is reached after 8 h and full strength after 24 h or after 30 min at 80 C. For permanent high-temperature resistance, WEICON recommends annealing at 80 C for 30 min after 24 h.

cureProfiles.condition: recommended anneal after 24 h for permanent high-temperature resistance

entries.potLifeConditions: 150 min at 20 C for a 10 ml batch; larger batches and higher temperatures shorten pot life.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: DIN EN 1465, 1.5 mm material thickness: steel 1.0338 sandblasted 20 MPa, aluminium sandblasted 15 MPa, ABS 6 MPa, PC 4 MPa, GFRP 10 MPa.
cautions: WEICON states continuous temperature resistance to 200 C and brief exposure only to 250 C.
Discard the first 5 cm from the static mixer before use.

Pot life and cure time are batch-size and temperature dependent; every 10 C increase above room temperature roughly halves them, temperatures below 16 C slow them significantly, and from about 5 C and below no reaction takes place.
For permanently high temperature resistance, anneal at 80 C for 30 min after 24 h room-temperature cure.
Store unopened cartridges dry at 18 to 25 C away from direct sunlight; failure to follow storage instructions can reduce shelf life to 6 months.
Epoxy resin crystallization below 5 C can harm processing/cure/technical data but may be reversed by heating up to max 50 C without open flame.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Light-gray viscous epoxy resin adhesive; 38,000 mPa.s at 20 C; density 2.1 g/cm3; 4 mm gap bridging; Shore D >90
entries.referenceForJoining: High-temperature metal and fiberglass bonding, powder-coated/thermo-coated assemblies after cure, metal construction, lightweight construction, shipbuilding and electrical engineering

entries.name: Flex 310 M Hybrid
entries.summary: Grey one-component SMP/MS-polymer adhesive sealant with exact WEICON TDS data: -40 to 90 C temperature resistance, 5 mm gap bridging, 10 min skin-over, 2-3 mm/24 h cure speed, 1.4 MPa medium lap shear, 1.3 MPa tensile strength, 500% elongation and Shore A 32 hardness.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: WEICON Flex 310 M Hybrid Technical Data Sheet, issue date April 23, 2026
entries.chemistry: One-component MS-polymer / SMP adhesive and sealant
entries.cureDetail: One-component neutral cross-linking moisture-cure MS-polymer adhesive/sealant. Processing and cure conditions are +5 to +40 C and 30% to 95% relative humidity; parts must be joined before the 10 min skin-over time.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Medium lap shear strength according to DIN EN 1465 / ASTM D1002 from WEICON TDS.
cautions: This is an elastic adhesive/sealant, not a rigid structural epoxy; the TDS lap-shear value is 1.4 MPa.
Bonding parts must be joined before the adhesive skins over.
Surfaces must be clean and free of grease; WEICON recommends Surface Cleaner, Cleaner Spray S for heavily soiled metal, and Sealant and Adhesive Remover for old paint or adhesive residues.
For some materials or special requirements, WEICON recommends a bonding agent / primer, and mechanical roughening can improve adhesion.
Store unopened at room temperature and avoid heat sources and direct sunlight.
The TDS values are laboratory/practical-experience recommendations, not guaranteed product characteristics.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Grey pasty 1C MS-polymer; density 1.5 g/cm3; skin-over 10 min; 2-3 mm/24 h cure speed; Shore A 32; 5 mm gap bridging
entries.referenceForJoining: Powder-coated, galvanised, anodised, chromated and hot-dip galvanised metal, plastics, natural stone, concrete, masonry, plaster, wooden floors and glass in tank, apparatus, vehicle, pipeline, fittings, energy, electrical, insulation and plastics applications

entries.name: Flex 310 M HT 220+
entries.summary: Grey fast-curing one-component SMP/MS-polymer adhesive sealant with exact WEICON TDS data: -40 to 90 C continuous temperature resistance, short excursions of 120 min at 200 C or 30 min at 220 C, 10 min skin-over, 2-3 mm/24 h cure speed, 1.6 MPa lap shear, 3.4 MPa tensile strength, 160% elongation and Shore A 57 hardness.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: WEICON Flex 310 M HT 220+ Technical Data Sheet, issue date April 23, 2026
entries.chemistry: One-component MS-polymer / SMP adhesive and sealant
entries.cureDetail: One-component moisture-curing, neutral cross-linking MS-polymer adhesive/sealant. Processing and cure conditions are +5 to +40 C and 30% to 95% relative humidity; parts must be joined before the 10 min skin-over time.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Medium lap shear strength according to DIN EN 1465 / ASTM D1002 from WEICON TDS.
cautions: WEICON specifies +220 C only as a single approx. 30 min excursion and +200 C as 120 min; permanent temperature resistance is -40 to +90 C.
This is an elastic SMP adhesive/sealant, not a rigid structural epoxy; the TDS lap-shear value is 1.6 MPa.
Bonding parts must be joined before the adhesive skins over.
Surfaces must be clean and free of grease; WEICON recommends Surface Cleaner, Cleaner Spray S for heavily soiled metal, and Sealant and Adhesive Remover for old paint or adhesive residues.
For certain materials or special requirements, WEICON recommends a bonding agent / primer, and mechanical roughening can improve adhesion.

After artificial ageing according to DIN ISO 21194 Annex A, Flex 310 M HT 220+ with Primer K 200 or Activating Cleaner achieved grade 2 bead peel on aluminium and stainless steel, meaning 75% to 95% cohesive failure.
Store unopened at room temperature and avoid heat sources and direct sunlight.
The TDS values are laboratory/practical-experience recommendations, not guaranteed product characteristics.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Grey pasty 1C MS-polymer; density 1.5 g/cm3; skin-over 10 min; 2-3 mm/24 h cure speed; Shore A 57; stability/run-off <1 mm
entries.referenceForJoining: Elastic tolerance-bridging bonding and sealing in metal construction, tank and apparatus engineering, ventilation and air-conditioning systems, car body, container, wagon and vehicle construction, including components that will be powder-coated after bonding

entries.name: MONOPOX TC2270
entries.summary: Electrically insulating, thermally conductive one-part aluminum-nitride-filled epoxy for power semiconductor die attach and electronic assemblies requiring low-temperature heat cure, JEDEC MSL 1 reliability and high through-bond heat transfer.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: DELO MONOPOX TC2270 Technical Datasheet, as of 28.10.2024 07:58
entries.chemistry: Modified epoxy resin with aluminum nitride ceramic filler
entries.cureDetail: One-component heat-curing modified epoxy resin. DELO publishes typical cure times of 90 min at +60 C or 15 min at +80 C in an air convection oven; the specified curing temperature must be reached directly at the adhesive.
entries.potLifeConditions: DELO TDS calls this a 48 h processing time at +23 C / 50% RH for the one-component product; it is not a mixed two-part pot life.
entries.volumeResistivity: >1E14 Ohm.cm by DIN EN 62631-3-1 after 80 C x 30 min cure
entries.viscosityUnit: mPa.s at 10 1/s shear rate, 500 um gap
entries.mslRating: JEDEC MSL 1 referring to IPC/JEDEC J-STD-020D.1
entries.appearance: Gray opaque cured material in 1 mm layer thickness
entries.chipBondStrength: 60 N die shear on 1 mm x 1 mm silicon die on gold after 80 C x 30 min cure; 62 N on silicon die to annealed FR4 after the same cure.
entries.hardnessScale: Shore D, DIN EN ISO 868 after 80 C x 30 min cure
standards: JEDEC MSL 1 referring to IPC/JEDEC J-STD-020D.1
DIN EN ISO 868 Shore D hardness
cautions: The official TDS reports DELO compression shear and die-shear values, not a standard lap-shear value; lapShear is intentionally left blank.
The 48 h processing-time value is captured as potLife only as a one-component processing-life equivalent, not as a two-component mixed pot life.
Heating time of the components must be added to the published cure time, and the specified cure temperature must be reached directly at the adhesive.
Changing curing temperature, irradiation intensity or cure time can change physical properties.
Depending on adhesive quantity, exothermic reaction heat can cause overheating; DELO advises selecting a lower cure temperature in that case.
The Technical Datasheet states values are typical laboratory values and are not a product specification.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Gray opaque aluminum-nitride-filled modified epoxy paste; 145,000 mPa.s at 10 1/s; density 2.15 g/cm3; thixotropy index 4.5; 88 Shore D after 80 C x 30 min cure
entries.referenceForJoining: Power-semiconductor die attach, silicon die to gold or FR4, thermally conductive electrically insulating PCB and LCP/ABS electronics assemblies

entries.name: Elecolit 325
entries.summary: Silver-filled two-part solvent-free modified epoxy for electrical contacting and ESD discharge, with 2 h pot life, room-temperature or heat cure, 3.8-4.2 W/m*K thermal conductivity and 1E-4 to 5E-4 Ohm.cm volume resistivity.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Hoenle/Panacol Elecolit 325 Technical Datasheet, updated September 11, 2025, revision 6
entries.cureFamily: Electrically and thermally conductive two-part epoxy
entries.cureDetail: Mix two components at 1:1 ratio and apply within the 2 h pot life. Typical cure schedules are 16 h at 25 C, 2 h at 50 C, 30 min at 100 C, 15 min at 120 C or 5 min at 150 C. Final bond strength is achieved no sooner than 24 h after bonded components are removed from the oven.
entries.viscosityUnit: mixed adhesive; Panacol TDS lists paste-like rather than numeric mPa.s
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Steel/steel lap shear from Elecolit 325 TDS: 5-10 MPa after room-temperature 24 h cure; 15-20 MPa after 120 C / 15 min cure.
cautions: Electrically conductive ICA material intended for electronic contacting/ESD paths, not insulating bonding.
After thawing once, Elecolit 325 should not be frozen again.
After storage at 0-10 C, homogenize because silver sedimentation is possible.
Avoid condensation during thawing; 30 cc and 50 cc syringes need approximately 1 h thaw time.
Low-surface-energy substrates such as polyethylene and polypropylene must be pretreated.
Published values are typical properties and not product specifications.
entries.referenceCategory: Electrically and Thermally Conductive Epoxy Adhesives
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Grey paste-like 1:1 epoxy; 2 h pot life; 71 wt% silver filler; density 2.5-2.7 g/cm3; Shore D 70-85; thermal conductivity 3.8-4.2 W/m*K; volume resistivity 1E-4 to 5E-4 Ohm.cm
entries.referenceForJoining: Electrical contacting, ESD protection/discharge and thermally conductive bonding of heat-sensitive electronic components where a room-temperature or heat-curable conductive epoxy is required

entries.name: 1530C
entries.summary: Translucent one-component elastomeric adhesive for general-purpose industrial bonding where flexibility and moisture cure are preferred over rigid bondlines.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: ThreeBond International 1500 Series Elastic & Bonding Adhesives product page, cached April 24, 2026
entries.cureDetail: Single-component moisture-curing alcohol-type elastomeric adhesive. The 1530-series description states strong initial tack develops in 5-10 minutes, practical strength can reach about half final strength after 12-24 h depending on bond area, and final strength is reached after 3-7 days.
entries.workingStrengthHours: 12-24 h to about half final strength depending on bonding area

fullCureProfiles.context: approx. practical strength / one-half final strength, bond-area dependent

entries.lapShearSubstrate: Iron/iron lap shear strength for TB1530C on ThreeBond 1500 Series product page: 3.5 MPa.
cautions: ThreeBond states product-table values are examples of measured values and not guarantee levels.
Before using, confirm adequacy and safety for the relevant application.
The source product page gives product-family data rather than a full downloadable product-specific TDS for 1530C.
The product page does not publish service-temperature minimum, pot life, thermal conductivity, dielectric properties, chemical resistance or shelf life.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Translucent elastomeric adhesive (100 Pa.s)
entries.referenceForJoining: General-purpose elastic bonding across industrial materials

entries.name: Dowsil Q3-3526
entries.summary: Fast-curing non-slump two-part silicone adhesive/sealant with exact Dow selection-guide data: -50 to 190 C continuous temperature range, 8 min skin-over, 20 min tack-free, <2 mm flow, Shore A 38-40, 2 MPa tensile strength, 270-280% elongation, 1.32-1.36 specific gravity range and excellent primerless adhesion to nylon, polycarbonate, glass, aluminum, copper and common steels.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: DOWSIL Silicone Sealants and Foams for Industrial, Appliance and Maintenance selection guide, Form No. 80-8283-01
entries.chemistry: Two-component neutral silicone adhesive/sealant, VMQ sealant type
entries.cureDetail: Two-component neutral silicone adhesive/sealant; fast cure at room temperature with through-cure rather than outside-inward moisture cure. Dow table lists 8 min skin-over and 20 min tack-free time.
cautions: The official Dow selection guide publishes tensile strength but not lap-shear strength for Q3-3526, so lapShear is intentionally omitted.
The guide does not publish thermal conductivity or pot life for Q3-3526.
Most paints will not adhere to silicone sealant.
Not for underwater structural or adhesive applications.
Some plastics may stress-crack; test before use.
Service-temperature ranges are estimated from formulation and laboratory testing; actual service range depends on the application environment.
Primerless adhesion entries marked with an asterisk in the Dow table are encoded as consult Dow rather than treated as positive ratings.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Fast-curing non-slump two-part silicone adhesive/sealant; <2 mm flow; Shore A 38-40; gray or black
entries.referenceForJoining: Automotive headlights and auxiliary lights, body panels and body components, oven door windows and other appliance components, especially glass, polycarbonate, aluminum, copper and steel assemblies

entries.name: Dowsil EA-9187L
entries.summary: One-part translucent solventless UV-indicated silicone conformal coating for high-reliability electronics, with exact Dow TDS values: 900 mPa.s viscosity, 9 min skin-over, 0.44 MPa tensile strength, 165% elongation, 0.31 MPa unprimed glass lap shear, 20 kV/mm dielectric strength, 3E15 ohm.cm volume resistivity and UL 746C / UL 94 V-0 recognition.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Dow DOWSIL EA-9187L UV Silicone Conformal Coating Technical Data Sheet, Form No. 11-3909-01, 2018
entries.chemistry: One-part neutral-cure RTV silicone conformal coating
entries.cureDetail: Flowable one-component neutral-cure RTV silicone conformal coating. Cures with atmospheric moisture at room temperature, with optional mild heat acceleration of 60 C or less; adhesion may take up to 72 h at room temperature to build.

cureProfiles.context: room-temperature adhesion/full-property build, up to 72 h
optional mild heat acceleration maximum; time not published

entries.lapShearSubstrate: Unprimed lap shear on glass from Dow TDS.
reworkProfiles: Can be removed from substrates and circuitry by scraping, cutting, solvents or stripping agents.
A soldering iron may be applied directly through the coating to remove a single circuit component with proper ventilation, then the area can be cleaned, dried and recoated.
cautions: This is a conformal coating / thin-section encapsulant rather than a gap-filling structural adhesive.
Cure depends on method of application, film thickness, temperature and humidity; mild heat at 60 C or less can improve cure times.
Pot life depends on application method; to extend pot life, minimize exposure to ambient moisture with dry air or dry nitrogen blanketing when possible.
Adhesion can lag behind cure and may take up to 72 h at room temperature; test adhesion only after that interval.
Apply to clean, dry substrates; difficult low-surface-energy substrates may need primer, chemical etching or plasma treatment.
Store in original packaging tightly closed, minimize headspace, and prevent moisture contact; purge partially filled containers with dry air or nitrogen.

Useful temperature range is application-dependent; Dow states most silicone adhesives are operational -45 to 200 C for long periods, with possible low-temperature cycling to -55 C only after assembly verification.
Not tested or represented as suitable for medical or pharmaceutical uses.
TDS values are typical and not intended for preparing specifications.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Translucent one-part flowable neutral-cure RTV silicone; 900 mPa.s; skin-over 9 min at 25 C; Shore A 17; specific gravity 0.98
entries.referenceForJoining: Printed circuit board assemblies, electrical/electronic products and components requiring dielectric protection, corrosion resistance, environmental protection, thin-section encapsulation, UL 746C and UL 94 V-0

entries.summary: Low-viscosity multipurpose silicone liquid gasket for general sealing and gasketing where flow into narrow joints is useful.

entries.cureDetail: Room-temperature-vulcanizing silicone liquid gasket; curing condition is moisture cure. ThreeBond positions TB1211 as a multipurpose low-viscosity silicone-based liquid gasket with excellent oil resistance for use with solid sheet gaskets.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: The 1200-series product page does not publish lap shear for TB1211; it publishes blow-out resistance of 10 MPa and higher at room temperature.
cautions: ThreeBond states product-table values are examples of measured values and not guarantee levels.
Best fit is sealing and liquid-gasket use, not structural load transfer.
Current source exposes tack-free time and blow-out resistance but not a lap-shear strength value.
The source product page does not publish service-temperature range, pot life, thermal conductivity, dielectric properties, chemical resistance or shelf life for TB1211.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: White silicone liquid gasket; 68 Pa.s

entries.summary: Red medium-strength anaerobic sealant/threadlocker with exact ThreeBond TDS data: 650 mPa.s viscosity, 1.13 specific gravity, 2 h workable cure speed, 24 h final cure, 0.3 mm maximum clearance, 28 MPa fixing shear strength and 22 N.m steel M10 breakaway torque.

entries.cureDetail: One-part anaerobic adhesive for threaded metal assemblies. Workable cure speed is 2 h and final cure is 24 h; heat cure options are 80 C for 30 min, 100 C for 20 min or 120 C for 10 min.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Steel fixing shear strength 28 MPa (290 kgf/cm²) from TB1324 TDS
cautions: Anaerobic adhesive requires metallic contact and oxygen exclusion for cure; it remains uncured in open air.
Torque and shear values are fixture/test-geometry dependent M10 and ThreeBond shear-strength values, not general lap-shear values.
The TDS application sentence says small-diameter removable threaded fasteners, while the current ThreeBond product table positions TB1324 for M10 or larger bolts.
Typical values only, not product specifications.
The TDS does not publish dielectric properties, peel strength, thermal conductivity, shelf life or storage temperature.

entries.name: Vitralit 6127
entries.summary: Low-viscosity transparent one-part UV-curing modified acrylate for static glass and optical-component bonding, with capillary action, secondary heat/activator cure options, 20-100 mPa.s viscosity, 17-21 kV/mm dielectric strength and 12-17 MPa glass/aluminum lap shear.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Hoenle/Panacol Vitralit 6127 Technical Datasheet, updated September 15, 2025, revision 6
entries.cureDetail: One-part solvent-free modified acrylate. UV-A discharge-lamp cure is 15 s at 60 mW/cm2 through a 0.05 mm layer; 365 nm LED cure is 10 s at 300 mW/cm2 through a 0.05 mm layer. Secondary heat cure is 40 min at 120 C, and chemical cure with activator is 25 min.
entries.viscosityUnit: mPa.s, Brookfield LVT at 25 C, spindle 2, 60 rpm, test instruction P001
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Glass/aluminum lap shear range from Vitralit 6127 TDS, UV-A Fe-doped hand lamp at 60 mW/cm2 for 30 s. Compression shear strength glass/glass is 14-18 MPa.
cautions: At least one substrate must be transparent to the recommended wavelength for full UV cure.
Heat curing may only be used as a secondary process for shadowed areas when UV curing is applicable.
Light-sensitive uncured adhesive can begin curing in daylight or longer-term indoor lighting; use 100% light-blocking fluid lines and dispense tips.
Low-surface-energy substrates such as polyethylene and polypropylene must be pretreated for sufficient adhesion.
Published values are typical properties and not product specifications.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Transparent acrylate liquid; 20-100 mPa.s; density 1.1-1.15 g/cm3; refractive index 1.47-1.48; Shore D 70-80; operating -40 to 125 C; 0.1-0.3 W/m*K thermal conductivity; 17-21 kV/mm dielectric strength
entries.referenceForJoining: Static glass-object bonding and optical-component bonding where low viscosity, capillary action, clear bondline, good metal adhesion and UV/secondary cure options are required

entries.name: MA300
entries.summary: Two-part 1:1 methacrylate structural adhesive for thermoplastic, metal, and composite assemblies, supplied as a non-sagging gel with published 0.25-3.2 mm gap tolerance.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: ITW Performance Polymers Plexus MA300 TDS, last update January 17, 2025
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by volume and weight; working time 3-6 minutes at 23 C; reaches about 500 psi in 11-13 minutes and 1000 psi in 13-16 minutes at 23 C
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Aluminum, ASTM D1002, adhesive failure
cautions: The TDS gives temperature resistance as a strength-retention graph only; no continuous service temperature range is published.
PC120 Cleaner Conditioner is recommended on metal per the TDS before relying on the headline metal-bond values.
Application at 65-85 F / 18-30 C is recommended; colder or hotter temperatures significantly change cure rate.
Bulk dispensing equipment must be explosion proof; wetted parts should be stainless steel and should avoid brass, carbon steel, copper/copper alloys, Viton, BUNA-N and Neoprene.
Large mixed masses can generate substantial exotherm; dispense only what can be used within working time and keep gaps within max fill.
Chemical resistance is susceptible to strong polar solvents, strong acids and bases.
entries.referenceSampleType: 50 ml / 400 ml cartridge, pail, or drum
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Non-sagging gel; component viscosity 40-70 cP x1000 for both parts
entries.referenceForJoining: Thermoplastics, metals, composites, gelcoat, ABS, polycarbonate, PVC, and structural mixed-material assemblies
entries.serviceTemperatureNote: TDS explicitly recommends application at 65–85°F (18–30°C) for proper cure; temperatures outside that range slow or accelerate cure significantly.

entries.name: MA1025
entries.summary: Low-odor, low-shrink two-part 10:1 methacrylate adhesive for structural bonding in marine and composite assemblies with bond lines up to 25 mm thick.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Plexus MA1025 Technical Data Sheet Rev 09, May 2018
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 by volume; working time 20-25 minutes; fixture time 40-45 minutes at 23 C
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Cohesive strength ASTM D1002, 8.3-13.8 MPa (1200-2000 psi); no specific substrate listed
cautions: The official sheet reports tensile and lap-shear as ranges, so those metrics remain in text rather than being collapsed into synthetic point estimates.
Chemical resistance notes explicitly say the product is susceptible to polar solvents and strong acids or bases.
This product is designed around thick marine bond lines and large fiberglass assemblies rather than thin precision joints.
Plexus adhesive Part A is flammable and contains methacrylate esters; keep away from heat, sparks and open flames.
Large mixed masses can generate substantial exotherm and release entrapped air, steam or volatile gases; dispense only what can be used within working time and keep gaps within max fill.
Application at 65-85 F / 18-30 C is recommended; colder or hotter temperatures significantly change cure rate.
Avoid copper/copper alloys in dispensing contact parts; use stainless steel wetted parts and avoid Viton, BUNA-N, Neoprene and other elastomer seals.
ITW recommends substrate testing under anticipated service conditions.

entries.cureCondition: Cured-property table: 7 days / 25 C. Application details: cures at room temperature when exposed to air humidity.

fullCureProfiles.condition: room temperature with free access to atmospheric moisture

cautions: RTV-1 silicones require free access to air moisture; total through-cure rate is limited by moisture diffusion through the silicone rubber.
Heat curing is recommended only for films below 0.5 mm thickness because rapid acetic-acid release can cause blistering in thicker sections.
Acetic acid is liberated during cure and disappears when cure is complete.
Substrates should be clean; primers are available for special adhesion problems.
Cured silicone must be removed mechanically or with swelling/chemical silicone removers; clean equipment before full vulcanization where possible.
The checked WACKER TDS/product page does not publish numeric service range, pot life, lap-shear strength or thermal conductivity.

cautions: Catalyst choice changes working life and cure behavior; WACKER does not publish one universal pot life for all SEMICOSIL 811 catalyst combinations.
Direct WACKER TDS values were used for cured properties; lap-shear values come from WACKER's official SEMICOSIL 811 application sheet under UV-assisted cure.
Cure can be inhibited by sulfur-containing materials, plasticizers, urethanes, amines and organometallic compounds, especially organotin compounds.
If substrate inhibition is unknown, run a small-scale compatibility test.
Surfaces must be clean, dry and free of dust, salts, processing aids and greases.

entries.summary: One-component oxime condensation-cure Shin-Etsu liquid silicone rubber adhesive/sealant for general industrial bonding and sealing, with exact official KE-45 TDS values: -40 to 180 C continuous use, 6 min tack-free time, 1.04 g/cm3 density, Shore A 30, 2.0 MPa tensile strength, 350% elongation, 23 kV/mm dielectric breakdown, 0.21 W/m.K thermal conductivity and 1.0 MPa aluminum tensile lap-shear.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Shin-Etsu Silicone KE-45 TDS, English edited web version released November 2024; portal version date 2024/11/20 for KE-45-W
entries.chemistry: One-component room-temperature-curing oxime condensation silicone rubber
entries.cureDetail: One-component paste ready to use; cures from the surface inward by moisture-triggered oxime condensation at room temperature. Official recommended property-test cure condition is 23 +/- 2 C and 50 +/- 5% RH for 7 days.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Tensile lap-shear strength 1.0 MPa on aluminum from Shin-Etsu KE-45 TDS.
entries.packageSize: 100 g tube or 330 mL cartridge depending on color/variant
cautions: TDS values are not specified values and cannot be relied upon as product specifications.
Cures from the surface inward; Shin-Etsu says these products do not have particularly good deep-section cure properties and are not suitable for adhesive applications with large contact areas.
Do not cure where water droplets form above 100% humidity; hydrolysis may reduce strength or leave surface tack.
Oxime condensation products may corrode copper metals under sealed conditions; test suitability on the actual assembly.
Dielectric properties may temporarily decline during cure and recover after full cure.
Flux or certain other materials may inhibit cure, and the product should not be used where completely airtight conditions will be created.
Curing releases methyl ethyl ketoxime; use adequate ventilation and read the SDS.
Clean dirt, grime, moisture and oil from substrates before use.

entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 55,000-110,000 cP, Part B 30-70 cP; low-to-medium viscosity mixed
entries.referenceForJoining: Electronic, electrical, fiber-optic, optical and OEM applications with wide thermal excursions

entries.name: MasterSil 151
entries.summary: Two-part addition-cure optically clear silicone potting and encapsulation compound; 220-2,500 nm transmission with long pot life for LED and fiber-optic cladding applications.
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 by weight; 24-48 hours at 75 F or 1-2 hours at 200 F; 100% solids, no by-products released during cure
cautions: Addition-cure silicones are poisoned by amine, sulfur, tin, and some metal catalysts; qualify adjacent materials before potting.
Shore A 50-60 elastomer; not a structural adhesive, and peel-from-substrate is expected on smooth non-primed surfaces.
Optical transmission depends on bubble-free dispense; vacuum degas the mixed compound before potting optics.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 2,000-4,000 cP, Part B 50-150 cP; thin mixed consistency
entries.referenceForJoining: Encapsulating LEDs, optical fiber cladding, potting connectors, opto-electronic assemblies

entries.name: Supreme 11AOHT
entries.summary: Toughened thermally conductive, electrically insulating two-part epoxy with 1:1 mix and very wide -112 F to +400 F service range; good for bonding dissimilar substrates under thermal cycling.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by weight or volume; 8-12 hours at 75 F plus 1-2 hours at 150-200 F for optimum; 100 g open time about 20-30 minutes
entries.viscosityUnit: cP for Part A; Part B paste
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Tensile lap shear strength 3200-3400 psi (typical properties); stored as midpoint 22.8 MPa.
cautions: TDS lists Shore D 80-85 after full cure; rigid enough to transmit stress into brittle substrates if the stack is poorly designed.
Part A 200,000-300,000 cP and Part B paste: meter-mix equipment needs the right ratio-drive for this viscosity combination.
Thermally conductive / electrically insulative balance holds only if the thick-section cure schedule is followed.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 200,000-300,000 cP, Part B paste; toothpaste-like mixed
entries.referenceForJoining: Electronic, electro-optic, aerospace, and specialty OEM assemblies under thermal cycling

entries.name: EP21TDCHT-LO
entries.summary: Toughened two-part epoxy with forgiving 1:1 mix, NASA low-outgassing pass, and -100 F to +350 F service range for bonding dissimilar substrates under thermal cycling.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by weight; overnight at room temperature followed by 2-3 hours at 200 F, or straight heat cure at 150-200 F for 2-3 hours
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Tensile lap shear strength 2600-2800 psi (typical properties); stored as midpoint 18.6 MPa.
cautions: Passes NASA low-outgassing (ASTM E595) only when the recommended cure is followed; abbreviated schedules will not meet the requirement.
Low-outgassing designation is for vacuum/aerospace - general-purpose structural users do not need to pay for this grade.
Shore D 65-75 after cure: toughened but still rigid compared to flexible epoxies like E-120HP.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 60,000-110,000 cP, Part B 70,000-140,000 cP; moderate viscosity paste
entries.referenceForJoining: Aerospace, electronic, vacuum, semiconductor, and specialty OEM assemblies needing low outgassing and thermal-cycling tolerance

entries.summary: General-purpose two-part thixotropic epoxy paste with forgiving 1:1 mix and flexible cure (23 C / 24 hr to 100 C / 30 min); high-viscosity pre-cure holds parts in place without fixtures.

Weight loss at 300 C is 4.16% per TDS - not suited for vacuum/outgassing-sensitive applications.

entries.referenceForJoining: Job shop / repair, outdoor and recreational bonding, SMD-to-PCB staking, LCD plug seal, X-ray sensor fabrication on lead, wood-working

entries.summary: High-Tg fiber-optic grade two-part epoxy with 1:1 mix and 24-hour pot life; NASA low-outgassing approved, suited for MEMS spin-coating and hermetic LCD glass seals.

cautions: 24-hour pot life is unusually long for an epoxy; mixed material still needs the 150 C / 1 hour heat cure to develop properties.

cautions: Thermal conductivity is geometry-dependent: 1.5 W/mK at 3 mil bond line, 0.7 W/mK at 40 mil; design the joint for the thin regime to get the headline number.

High-temperature resistance above the hot-strength chart is only stated for brief excursions, so no continuous maximum service temperature is encoded.

Do not count automotive sensor-housing bonding as electronics-performance evidence; the TDS does not publish electrical, dielectric, thermal-interface, potting, conformal-coating, conductive-adhesive, or die-attach properties.

entries.name: 3012D
entries.summary: Flexible medium-viscosity blue UV-curable electronics potting and encapsulating adhesive with a secondary 150 C thermal-cure path for deeper or shadowed sections.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Three Bond International TB 3012D Technical Data Sheet Rev. 0.0, issued 05/20/2011, document control 3-TB-20-1252
entries.cureDetail: One-part UV-curable adhesive. Standard curing condition is 3000 mJ/cm2 using a Fusion D bulb / doped mercury source; UV-only curing thickness is 1.05 mm and UV plus 10 min at 150 C cures >5 mm.
entries.depthCurabilityMm: >5 with UV plus 10 min at 150 C

uvFixtureProfiles.condition: Typical suitable high-pressure mercury or mercury-metal-halide UV source at 500-1000 mW/cm2 UV-A/B region

cautions: The TDS does not publish thermal conductivity or a continuous service-temperature range.
Designed for potting and encapsulation rather than highly loaded structural joints.
Shadowed areas need the secondary heat-cure path to fully polymerize.
Bonding surfaces must be free of water, oil and other contaminants.
Use worker safety equipment to shield skin and eyes from stray UV light.
If resin is transferred to another container, ThreeBond specifies an opaque UV-blocking polyethylene or polypropylene container; metal and glass containers are not recommended.
Transferred resin should not be returned to the original container.
The product can polymerize when exposed to UV-A/B radiation and also above 60 C; higher temperature increases polymerization rate.
Do not freeze samples; store sealed in the original container at room-temperature conditions.
Published values are typical results and should be confirmed by lab and actual trial runs.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Blue liquid; 9600 cP; specific gravity 1.1; 45 Shore A after cure; 3000 mJ/cm2 standard UV dose
entries.referenceForJoining: Potting and encapsulating electronic assemblies where flexibility and heat resistance are needed

entries.name: 3164F

entries.name: 3303N
entries.summary: Heat-curing one-part silicone-based conductive adhesive for SMD quartz crystals and other small electronic components that need conductive fixing over a wide temperature range.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Three Bond Co., Ltd. ThreeBond 3303N Technical Data, October 6, 2005, document ED070403
entries.cureDetail: After applying and bonding the adherend, cure at 180 C for 60 minutes in a hot-air drying chamber. For degassing and stress relieving, the TDS recommends further aging at 200 C or more.

postCureProfiles.context: recommended further aging for degassing and stress relieving

entries.viscosityUnit: Pa.s by 3TS-210-05; EHD-type viscometer, 0.5 rpm, rotor 1 deg 34 min for 24 h
entries.lapShearSubstrate: The official TB3303N technical data does not publish lap shear; it publishes chip bonding strength of 3.1 MPa on a ceramic chip to glass sheet after 180 C x 60 min cure.
entries.volumeResistivity: 2.3E-6 Ohm.m after 180 C x 60 min cure
entries.chipBondStrength: 3.1 MPa, ceramic chip / glass sheet after 180 C x 60 min cure
cautions: Conductive attachment adhesive for electronics assembly, not a general-purpose structural bonder.
Recommended cure is 180 C for 60 minutes with optional higher-temperature aging for degassing and stress relief.
Open the refrigerated container only after it reaches room temperature to prevent condensation.
Conductive filler may settle during storage; sufficiently stir before use to uniformly mix the filler.
Curing failure may occur if the adhesive contacts or is mixed with catalytic poisons including water, sulfur, phosphorus, nitrogen compounds and organic metal salts.
Use a syringe or applicator nozzle inner diameter of 0.21 mm / 27G or more.
The TDS gives actual measurements, not guaranteed values.

The TDS does not publish density or specific gravity, lap shear, peel, tensile strength, elongation, surface resistivity, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, Tg, CTE, shelf life or service-temperature range.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Light yellow silicone-based conductive paste; 41 Pa.s
entries.referenceForJoining: SMD quartz crystals, crystal oscillators, SAW filters, chip components and conductive spot bonding in electronics

entries.name: 3315E

dryingProfiles.condition: Published shear and resistivity drying condition

entries.bondingConditions: For peel test, dried film was heat-bonded at 200 C x 10 sec x 0.49 MPa with 20 um film thickness.
entries.solventContents: TDS permits dilution only with toluene or xylene; do not dilute or mix with other organic solvents or substances.
cautions: The TDS does not publish thermal conductivity or a continuous service-temperature range.
Intended for conductive film formation and heat bonding, not bulk gap filling or conventional structural repair.
Solvent evaporation and dry-film formation are part of the process before final heat bonding.
Combustible substance; do not use near fire.
Harmful to health; do not touch directly or inhale vapor.
Use mask, impervious gloves, goggles and local exhaust or outdoor ventilation.
People with allergies or sensitive skin should avoid using it.
Do not pour into other containers or return unused adhesive to its original container.
Components may separate during storage; stir before use until uniform.
Some materials may deteriorate if this product is used; pre-test affected parts.
Store capped in a dark dry place away from direct sunlight and use within six months.
Industrial use only; not for household products or medical implants.

entries.lapShearSubstrate: lap shear not stated; official torque strength test on pre-oxidized 1/2-13 nut and bolt after final cure at 1000 F
cautions: Official bulletin does not publish lap shear, tensile, compressive, hardness, thermal conductivity or a numeric minimum service temperature for this grade.
Dielectric strength conversion uses 171 V/mil from the official table, equal to about 6.73 kV/mm.
Aremco notes relatively low mechanical strength and slight porosity after cure for this adhesive family.
Fast water-vapor release can blister during cure; use about 5 F/min ramp and slow down if blistering occurs.
Viscosity may be adjusted by thinning up to 20% by weight with the listed water thinner.
Design for CTE mismatch and compression loading; target glue line is 2-8 mil.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: White one-part water-based ceramic paste; 50,000-90,000 cP
entries.referenceForJoining: Alumina-to-alumina dense ceramic bonding, ceramic assemblies and high-temperature electrical insulation

entries.name: Ceramabond 571
entries.summary: Two-part magnesia-filled inorganic ceramic adhesive for ceramic-to-metal and metal-to-metal high-temperature heater, induction coil and sensor assemblies up to 1760 C.
entries.cureDetail: Two-part magnesia-filled water-based ceramic adhesive; powder/liquid mix, air sets in 1-4 hours and heat cures 2 hours at 200 F
entries.lapShearSubstrate: lap shear not stated; official torque strength test on pre-oxidized 1/2-13 nut and bolt after final cure at 1000 F
cautions: Official bulletin does not publish lap shear, tensile, compressive, hardness, thermal conductivity or a numeric minimum service temperature for this grade.
Dielectric strength conversion uses 91 V/mil from the official table, equal to about 3.58 kV/mm.
Aremco notes relatively low mechanical strength and slight porosity after cure for this adhesive family.
Fast water-vapor release can blister during cure; use about 5 F/min ramp and slow down if blistering occurs.
Viscosity and specific gravity ranges reflect powder-to-liquid mix ratios from 1:1 to 1.5:1.
Viscosity may be adjusted by thinning up to 20% by weight with the listed water thinner.
Design for CTE mismatch and compression loading; target glue line is 2-8 mil.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Off-white two-part water-based ceramic paste; 20,000-90,000 cP depending powder/liquid ratio
entries.referenceForJoining: Ceramic-to-metal and metal-to-metal heater, induction coil, sensor and high-temperature electrical assemblies

entries.name: Pyro-Putty 653
entries.summary: One-part stainless-filled ceramic-metallic water-based repair paste for cast iron, steel and stainless parts up to 1093 C, applied vertically up to 1/2 inch thick.
entries.cureDetail: One-part stainless-filled water-based paste; air set 2-4 hours and heat cure 3-4 hours at 200 F
cautions: Exact Aremco TDS for suffix 653-CE was not found; this entry uses the nearest official Pyro-Putty 653 bulletin and preserves the non-exact evidence in blocked targets.
Official bulletin does not publish bond, shear, compressive, tensile, thermal conductivity, electrical data, hardness or CTE values for this grade.
Use multiple applications for cross sections over 1/8-1/4 inch to reduce blistering.
Surfaces must be oil-free and abrasive-blasted where appropriate; bake porous castings to remove embedded oils.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Metallic gray one-part stainless-filled water-based paste
entries.referenceForJoining: Cast iron, steel and stainless repair/seal applications such as afterburners, boilers, exhaust stacks, manifolds, molds, dies and heat exchangers

entries.name: SNAPSIL RTV162
entries.summary: White one-part electronic-grade alkoxy RTV silicone adhesive sealant for sealing and bonding electronic components onto printed circuit boards and protecting copper connections.
entries.cureFamily: One-part neutral-cure RTV silicone adhesive sealant
entries.cureDetail: Cures on exposure to atmospheric moisture at room temperature with alcohol vapor byproduct; tack-free in about 4 hours at 25 C/50% RH; 3 mm section cures through in about 48 hours; cured-property data are after 7 days at 25 C/50% RH
entries.lapShearSubstrate: lap shear not stated; TDS gives peel strength on stainless steel screen

cautions: The TDS does not publish lap shear, tensile modulus or bond-line shear test data; peel, tensile and electrical values are preserved instead.
Use section thicknesses of 6 mm or less; broad-surface bonding should use a bead or ribbon less than 6 mm around the edge.
Do not tool after the tack-free skin begins to form.
For difficult substrates use SS4004, SS4044 or SS4179 primer after cleaning and drying the surface.
Solvent cleaning and uncured sealant handling require the latest SDS and normal solvent safety controls.

cautions: This is a primer used with 3M Scotch-Weld instant adhesives, not a standalone structural adhesive.
The TDS warns overspray or excess may attack certain substrates, especially some plastics, causing unsightly effects.
AC79 is acetone based; evaluate elastomers and adjacent plastics for solvent attack before production use.
The TDS does not publish fixture time, dry time, lap shear, peel, dielectric, thermal, tensile, hardness or service-temperature properties.
AC11 and AC452 are marked not available in the Americas Region in the 2015 TDS; AC79 is represented by the Digi-Key AC79 8 oz row.
entries.priceExample: Digi-Key 3M160992-ND / AC79 8 oz bottle, observed from glue__adhesives__applicators.csv
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Clear low-viscosity acetone-based liquid primer; 2 cP viscosity and 0.7 g/mL specific gravity
entries.referenceForJoining: Primer treatment for elastomeric materials including silicone before Scotch-Weld cyanoacrylate bonding
entries.serviceTemperatureNote: Storage temperature 16–27 °C per TDS; these are CA primers/activators; no service temperature rating applies to primer film.

entries.name: Plastic Bonding Adhesive 2665B
entries.summary: Black one-component moisture-curing hot-melt urethane adhesive with PSA-like tack for cover-glass and plastic-to-plastic bonding in electronic-device assemblies.
entries.cureDetail: Dispense at 110 C; molten open time is 1.5-4 minutes for a 1.5 mm bead on plastic. After solidification the adhesive has PSA-like open time of 1-30 minutes, with optimal PSA open time 10-20 minutes. Cure rate depends on air temperature, relative humidity, substrate and bond-line thickness.
entries.viscosityUnit: cP at 110 C / 230 F, Brookfield Thermosel spindle #27 at 10 rpm
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Polycarbonate overlap shear at 23 C after cure, ASTM D1002-72; 5.9 MPa
entries.peelStrength: Initial peel strength 1.3 N/mm / 7.5 lb/in, 5 minutes after lamination with 10 minute open time, ASTM D3330 Method A, 5 mil polycarbonate film to polycarbonate coupon
cautions: The serviceMin/serviceMax scalars reflect thermal-shock test extremes, not a continuous service-temperature specification.
The TDS says 2665B is not recommended for bonding untreated polyolefins.
Hot syringes are a burn or rupture hazard; do not exceed 110 C syringe temperature or 4.1 bar / 60 psi dispensing pressure.
Dry air is required for dispensing to prevent premature curing, and glass may need primer for hot/humid exposure.
The TDS does not publish dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, density, Tg or a fixed full-cure time.
entries.priceExample: Digi-Key 19-2665B-ND / 2665B case package, observed from glue__adhesives__applicators.csv; TDS package sizes include 30 mL syringes and 295 mL cartridges
entries.referenceCategory: Reactive Hot-Melt Urethane Plastic Bonding Adhesives
entries.referenceSampleType: 30 mL syringe or 295 mL cartridge; Digi-Key 2665B case package
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Black low-viscosity molten adhesive at 110 C with PSA-like tack after solidification
entries.referenceForJoining: Cover-glass bonding on electronic devices and plastic-to-plastic bonding, especially polycarbonate and acrylic assemblies

entries.name: Scotch-Weld PUR Adhesive 2710P / 2710p / 7100200889
entries.summary: Black one-component moisture-curing polyurethane reactive hot-melt adhesive with PSA-like tack, immediate handling strength after lamination, and rapid strength build for electronics plastic bonding.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M product page crawled 2026; SDS issue date September 2, 2021
entries.cureDetail: Official 3M product page lists 120 C application temperature, 1-4 minute open time, 1-4 minute set time and PSA-like immediate handling strength after lamination. Cure continues by moisture reaction.
entries.viscosityUnit: cP from 3M product-page typical properties; SDS also reports 9600 cP at 170 F
entries.lapShearSubstrate: The public 3M product page and SDS do not publish lap-shear values for 2710P.
cautions: No separate public 3M technical data sheet was located; this entry is backed by the official 3M product-page typical-property table plus the official 3M SDS physical-property table.
The public official sources do not publish lap shear, peel strength, tensile strength, hardness, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, Tg or service-temperature range.

Electronics relevance is based on the official product-page industry/category listing and plastic-bonding use; cached official sources do not publish adhesive electrical or thermal performance values for 2710P.
SDS hazards include respiratory sensitization, skin sensitization, repeated-exposure respiratory-system target-organ effects and thermal-burn risk when hot.
The adhesive is a hot material during application; SDS says to wear heat-insulating gloves and not attempt to remove molten material from skin or eyes.
Product-page viscosity and SDS viscosity differ in test condition, so the selector preserves both in the viscosityUnit note.
entries.priceExample: Digi-Key 19-2710P-ND / 2710P 30 mL, observed from glue__adhesives__applicators.csv
entries.referenceCategory: Reactive Hot-Melt Urethane Plastic Bonding Adhesives
entries.referenceSampleType: 30 mL black syringe; 3M ID 7100200889; Digi-Key 2710P row
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Black solid/waxy solid polyurethane reactive hot melt with PSA-like tack; 30 mL package
entries.referenceForJoining: Electronics plastic bonding where immediate PSA-like handling and rapid strength build are more important than a long open time

entries.name: Scotch-Weld Flexible Acrylic Adhesive DP8610NS / 7100244477
entries.summary: Low-odor non-flammable 10:1 black flexible acrylic structural adhesive with 200% elongation, ceramic spacer beads, thixotropic non-sag flow, 8 minute open time and 16-20 minute handling time.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M France-FR TDS V-8, last revision December 2025, supersedes September 2024
entries.chemistry: Two-part low-odor flexible acrylic structural adhesive
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 by volume and weight; 8 minute work/open time, 16-20 minute time to handling at 23 C to reach 0.35 MPa shear, and 24 hour final cure at 23 C. Materials and adhesive should be at least 16 C; heating up to 66 C accelerates cure.

fullCureProfiles.context: elevated temperature can accelerate cure; TDS does not publish a fixed time

entries.mixRatio: B:A 10:1 by volume; B:A 10:1 by weight
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Etched aluminum at 23 C after 24 h cure, ASTM D1002 / ISO 4587; 7 MPa
entries.peelStrength: Bell peel on etched aluminum after 24 h room-temperature cure: 128 N/cm / 12.8 N/mm, ASTM D3167
cautions: The TDS does not publish a continuous service-temperature range, so serviceMin/serviceMax are intentionally null; temperature shear data are test-point results.
The scalar gapFill uses the upper ceramic bead range because the TDS states ceramic beads from 0.050 to 0.250 mm control bondline thickness.
The product has relatively low adhesion on low-surface-energy plastics such as polypropylene, polyethylene, TPO and PTFE; end-user evaluation is required.
Oxygen inhibits acrylic structural adhesive reaction; exposed adhesive may remain tacky longer than adhesive inside the bondline.
All shear, peel and tensile values are typical and not for specification purposes in the TDS.

entries.name: Scotch-Weld Urethane Adhesive DP600 Self-Leveling and DP600NS / DP600 SELF-LEVELING-GRAY / DP600NS NON-SAG-GRAY / DP-600-SELF-LEVEL
entries.summary: Gray rapid-setting 1:1 two-part polyurethane adhesive family for self-leveling concrete repair, vertical non-sag repair, custom tapping, handrail anchoring, and tough non-brittle bonding of wood, metal, glass, plastic, concrete and masonry.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Primary 3M DP600 Self-Leveling TDS last revision September 2024, supersedes June 2024; legacy combined DP600/DP600NS TDS November 2016
entries.cureDetail: B:A 1:1 by volume and 1:1 by weight. Current DP600 Self-Leveling TDS lists 70 s worklife, 1 min open time, 4 min tack-free time and 1 h full cure at 23 C for a 10 g, 6 mm thick mass. The legacy combined TDS lists DP600NS worklife 50 s, tack-free 2 min and full cure 1 h.
entries.mixRatio: B:A 1:1 by volume; B:A 1:1 by weight
entries.viscosityValue: DP600 Self-Leveling Part B 1100-6000 cP, Part A 1000-3000 cP; DP600NS Part B 1500-3000 cP, Part A 1000-3000 cP
entries.viscosityUnit: cP, Brookfield CP #52 at 100 rpm, 23-25 C
entries.lapShearSubstrate: DP600 Self-Leveling aluminum MEK/abrade/MEK, 7 d at 23 C, ASTM D1002 / ISO 4587; 2300 psi / 15.9 MPa. DP600NS aluminum value is also 2300 psi in the legacy combined TDS.
entries.peelStrength: Floating roller / bell peel on etched aluminum at 23 C, ASTM D3167: DP600 Self-Leveling 20 lb/in width; DP600NS 9 lb/in width.
entries.hardnessScale: Shore D after 1 h at 23-25 C
cautions: This family is primarily a rapid repair/anchoring urethane, not a long-open-time assembly adhesive; at 23 C the formal worklife is under 75 seconds.
The scalar lapShear uses the shared aluminum 2300 psi value; exact DP600 and DP600NS substrate tables are preserved separately in shearStrengthsPsi and shearStrengthsMPa.

The current September 2024 PDF focuses on DP600 Self-Leveling and references DP600NS in directions; DP600NS-specific viscosity, worklife, tack-free, peel and environmental data come from the official November 2016 combined 3M TDS.
Custom tapping instructions say to insert the bolt or screw within the first 20 seconds after dispensing; do not use the 1 minute open time as a fastener-placement allowance.
The TDS does not publish tensile strength, elongation, dielectric strength, volume resistivity, thermal conductivity, Tg or CTE values.
3M says the product is not designed or tested for certain automotive electric powertrain battery or high-voltage applications.

entries.name: M-Bond 200 Strain Gage Adhesive / MMF006570
entries.summary: Certified cyanoacrylate strain-gage adhesive for routine experimental stress analysis under temperate conditions, with near-instant cure, creep-free fatigue-resistant bonds, and more than 5% elongation capability for many strain-gage installations.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: VPG Document No. 11010, revision 17-May-2017; legal disclaimer document No. 63999 revision 15-Jul-2014
entries.chemistry: Cyanoacrylate strain-gage adhesive used with M-Bond 200 catalyst
entries.cureDetail: One minute thumb pressure, then wait at least two minutes before tape removal. Bond strength increases rapidly during the first five minutes. Cure time must be extended below 70 F / 21 C or below 40% RH.
entries.potLifeConditions: Maximum 3 months at 75 F / 24 C after opening, not to exceed expiration date; replace cap immediately after each use.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: The M-Bond 200 strain-gage adhesive datasheet does not publish lap-shear strength; it specifies certified use for strain-gage bonding and elongation capability instead.
entries.strainGageElongationWithCeaOptionEPct: 3 at 75 F / 24 C when used with CEA or EA/Option E strain gages
cautions: The TDS says performance can be degraded by time, humidity, elevated temperature and moisture absorption; strain-gage installations should be protected with a suitable coating.
Use M-Bond epoxy adhesives instead for more rigorous environmental conditions or long-term field exposure.
Allow refrigerated adhesive to reach room temperature before opening; condensation rapidly degrades adhesive performance and shelf life.
Refrigeration after opening is not recommended.
The TDS does not publish viscosity, lap shear, peel, tensile strength, hardness, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, density, Tg or CTE values.
The serviceMin/serviceMax scalars use the long-term operating range; short-term -185 C to 95 C limits are preserved separately.
entries.priceExample: Digi-Key MMF006570 / M-Bond 200 bulk adhesive package: 16 one-ounce bottles observed at $996.90
entries.referenceSampleType: Bulk MMF006570 package with 16 bottles, each 1 oz / 28 g adhesive; TDS also lists kit, one-bottle, five-tube and catalyst packaging options
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Low-viscosity cyanoacrylate adhesive used with 30 mL brush-cap catalyst in the kit configuration
entries.referenceForJoining: Certified bonding of Micro-Measurements strain gages for laboratory and short-term field stress analysis under temperate environmental conditions

entries.name: Scotch Spray Mount Adhesive 6065 / 7100129195
entries.summary: Clear/translucent repositionable aerosol spray adhesive for short-term layout, poster, presentation, graphic-design, acetate, foil, fabric, light tissue and newsprint mounting where quick tack and non-wrinkling application matter more than engineering bond strength.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M product page observed 2026; SDS document group 37-0746-0 version 7.01, issue date May 30, 2023; RDS issue date February 1, 2024
entries.cureDetail: 3M product page describes quick tack; light spray allows repositioning while heavier spray gives a stronger bond. The SDS/product page do not publish a quantitative dry, set, open, fixture or full-cure time.
entries.viscosityUnit: cP at 73.4 F / 23 C from SDS Section 9
entries.lapShearSubstrate: 3M product page and SDS do not publish lap shear or peel strength for Scotch Spray Mount 6065.
entries.chemicalInventoryCompliance: SDS states components comply with TSCA notification requirements and required components are listed on the active TSCA Inventory.
cautions: This is a repositionable spray-mount adhesive, not an engineering structural adhesive; do not rank it by structural lap-shear strength.
The official 3M product page and SDS do not publish lap shear, peel, tensile, hardness, dielectric, thermal-conductivity, service-temperature range, dry time or full-cure values.
SDS hazards include extremely flammable aerosol, gas under pressure, serious eye irritation, skin sensitization, reproductive toxicity, simple asphyxiation and single-exposure target-organ effects.
SDS storage limits include protecting from sunlight and not exposing the aerosol can to temperatures above 50 C / 122 F.
3M states the product is not and cannot be distributed in commerce or processed for consumer paint or coating removal under TSCA definitions.
Product page states VOC less than 30% formulation; SDS Section 9 reports VOC less water and exempt solvents <=28%.
entries.priceExample: Digi-Key 19-6065-ND / 6065 box, observed from glue__adhesives__applicators.csv
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Clear to light-yellow solvent aerosol spray adhesive; translucent after application
entries.referenceForJoining: Temporary or repositionable mounting of layouts, posters, presentations, graphic-design materials, acetate, foils, fabrics, light tissues and newsprint

entries.name: Bondo Fiberglass Resin 0401 / 00401 / Liquid Resin for Fiberglass
entries.summary: Two-part high-strength polyester fiberglass resin for waterproof repair, sealing, rebuilding, recreating or joining fiberglass, metal, wood and masonry surfaces in auto, boat and home applications.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M Bondo Liquid Resin for Fiberglass TDS, December 2023; Digi-Key comparison chart also covers Bondo Fiberglass Resin 0401
entries.chemistry: Two-part polyester fiberglass resin with clear liquid hardener
entries.cureDetail: Working time is 8-12 minutes at 75 F / 24 C after resin and hardener are mixed. Cure/sand time is 2 hours at 75 F; cooler temperatures extend cure. A heat lamp may be used 18 in from the repair area for 15 min, but overheating can cause cracking.
entries.mixRatio: 1 oz resin to 10 drops liquid hardener; 6 tablespoons resin to 1/4 teaspoon hardener; for 0.9 pint use 1/4 can resin with 1/4 of a 0.37 oz hardener tube
entries.lapShearSubstrate: The Bondo Liquid Resin for Fiberglass TDS does not publish lap-shear strength; it describes repair and sealing use with fiberglass cloth or mat reinforcement.
cautions: This is a fiberglass/polyester repair resin, not an electronics adhesive or structural aerospace composite resin.
Use fiberglass cloth or mat reinforcement for holes and gaps in fiberglass or metal; resin alone is also described as a concrete or wood sealer for small areas.
Do not use on walking surfaces when used as a sealer because the cured product will be slippery.
Do not pour mixed resin back into the can because it will harden the rest of the contents.
The TDS warns cracking can occur if overheated during accelerated cure.
The TDS does not publish lap shear, peel, tensile strength, hardness, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, density, Tg, CTE, shelf life or continuous service-temperature range.
entries.priceExample: Digi-Key 19-0401-ND / 0401 Bondo Fiberglass Resin, 0.9 pint / 426 mL package observed at $32.79
entries.referenceSampleType: 0.9 pint / 426 mL 0401 container with included clear liquid hardener; broader TDS covers 0.9 pint, 0.9 quart and 0.9 gallon sizes
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Translucent light-brown cured polyester resin when mixed properly with clear liquid hardener
entries.referenceForJoining: Repairing chips, cracks, breaks, holes and gaps on fiberglass, metal, wood and masonry; rebuilding or joining surfaces; waterproofing and sealing small concrete or wood areas
