entries.name: Scotchcast Flame-Retardant Electrical Insulating Resin 2131 / 2131B
entries.summary: Black two-part flame-retardant semi-flexible polyurethane electrical insulating resin for 1 kV cable splice insulation, mine and portable cable jacket repair, potting printed circuit boards, junction boxes, back-shell connectors and motor repairs.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: 3M Scotchcast 2131 Data Sheet January 2015, 78-8129-9502-1 Rev D; 3M product page observed 2026
entries.chemistry: Two-part flame-retardant polyurethane electrical insulating resin
entries.cureDetail: Closed mixing pouch: premix, break barrier, mix 30 seconds, strip resin from corners and continue mixing until uniform for an additional 30 seconds maximum, then clip corner and pour. Bulk mix ratio is Part A:Part B 1:2.1 by weight or 1:1.69 by volume. Gel time is 17 minutes at 75 F / 23 C.
entries.mixRatio: Part A:Part B 1:2.1 by weight; 1:1.69 by volume
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Scotchcast 2131 is an electrical insulating and jacket-repair resin; the TDS publishes adhesion to metals and cable jackets rather than structural lap shear.
entries.flameRating: Flame-retardant; meets Part 7, Title 30 CFR/MSHA approvals when used in appropriate kit configuration
approvals: MSHA Part 7 Title 30 CFR in approved kit configurations
cautions: This is a permanent electrical insulating resin, not a re-enterable encapsulant; use Scotchcast 2123 for soft re-enterable applications.
De-energize and ground electrical systems before installation; TDS warns energized systems may cause serious injury or death.
In cold weather, warm the closed mixing pouch to at least 16 C / 60 F before mixing.
Opened bulk components should be blanketed with nitrogen to prevent moisture contamination.
The TDS does not publish lap shear, peel strength, thermal conductivity, volume resistivity, surface resistivity, CTE, chemical-resistance tables or a lower continuous service-temperature rating.
MSHA approvals apply when the resin is used in accordance with the listed 3M kit configurations, not automatically to arbitrary end-user assemblies.

entries.referenceForJoining: Replacing or repairing mine and portable cable jackets, insulating 1 kV cable splices, sealing crotches/sheaths, potting cables, wire encasements, cable fittings, printed circuit boards, junction boxes, back-shell connectors and motor repairs

entries.name: Scotch-Weld 2216 B/A Translucent / Clear / 7000046479
entries.summary: Injectable translucent flexible two-part room-temperature-curing epoxy adhesive with high peel strength, cryogenic etched-aluminum performance, long 120 minute work life and published electrical/thermal properties.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Official 3M 2216 B/A TDS, October 2018
entries.chemistry: Modified epoxy base with modified amine accelerator
entries.cureDetail: Mix 1:1 by weight or volume; application to substrates should be made within 120 minutes. Cure at 60 F / 16 C or above until firm; full cure is 30 days at 75 F / 24 C, 240 minutes at 150 F / 66 C, or 60 minutes at 200 F / 93 C.

entries.volumeResistivity: 3.0 x 10^12 ohm.cm at 73 F / 23 C and 500 V DC

The translucent variant has much lower etched-aluminum room-temperature lap shear than 2216 B/A Gray but the same 25 piw T-peel value.
The TDS does not publish translucent arc resistance, surface resistivity, outgassing, broad substrate shear table, tensile strength, elongation or gap-fill limits.
Technical data are representative or typical only and should not be used for specification purposes.

entries.name: Sikaflex-255 FC / 255-FC TUBE
entries.summary: Black fast-curing one-component moisture-cure polyurethane direct-glazing adhesive for commercial-vehicle mineral-glass windows and glass replacement, with long open time and Sika black-primerless bonding compatibility.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Sika USA Product Data Sheet version 03.01, April 2023
entries.cureDetail: Cures by reaction with atmospheric moisture; cure speed slows at low temperature or low humidity. Skin time is 40 minutes and open time is 20 minutes at 23 C / 50% RH; glass must always be installed within the open time.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Tensile lap-shear strength, CQP046-1 / ISO 4587; 4 MPa / 580 psi
entries.tearStrength: 12 N/mm / 70 pli tear propagation resistance, CQP045-1 / ISO 34
cautions: This is intended for experienced professional users in Transportation OEM and vehicle glass replacement markets.
Actual substrates and conditions must be tested to ensure adhesion and material compatibility.
Open time is significantly shorter in hot and humid climates; never install glass after the adhesive has built a skin.
Surface treatment depends on substrate and is crucial for a long-lasting bond; use the current Sika pre-treatment chart for one-component polyurethanes.
The PDS does not publish viscosity, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, full cure time or a fixed safe-drive-away time.

entries.cureDetail: Specimens are mixed for 1-2 minutes. Peel/solvent specimens are cured 45 minutes at 121 +/- 3 C; dielectric specimens are cured 2 hours at 95 +/- 3 C. Pot-life requirement is checked after 1 hour at 25 +/- 3 C followed by peel-strength test.

entries.name: TB1530
entries.summary: White one-component solventless moisture-curing elastomeric adhesive based on silyl-ended special polymer, with primerless adhesion to many substrates, electronics-friendly low cyclic siloxane content, and useful pressure-sensitive tack after about 7 minutes.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Three Bond International TB1530 TDS Rev 0.3, issued April 12, 2004, document control number 3-TB-20-0808
entries.chemistry: Silyl-ended special polymer one-component moisture-curing elastomeric adhesive
entries.cureDetail: Cures by atmospheric moisture. Standard property cure condition is 7 days at 23 C and 50% RH. Double-spread bonding is pressed after the adhesive becomes tacky after more than 7 minutes; strong temporary adhesion is available about 7-20 minutes after application. Single-spread bonding should be assembled within 5 minutes.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Aluminum lap shear after 7 days at 23 C / 50% RH by 3TS-301-13: 6.6 MPa / 67 kgf/cm2, cohesive failure.
entries.peelStrength: T-peel after 7 days at 23 C / 50% RH with 5 minute open time by 3TS-304-23: aluminum 2.5 kN/m, canvas 1.8, NBR 1.6, CR 1.4, SBR 1.4, NR 1.8, EPDM 0.83, silicone rubber 0.30.
cautions: The TDS states the listed data are typical properties, not product specifications.
TB1530 has a limited tack-stage assembly window; if the double-spread joint is left longer than about 20 minutes after application, strong adhesion cannot be obtained without reapplication.
The TDS publishes shear-vs-temperature, heat-cycle, thermal-resistance and moisture-resistance graphs but not tabulated numeric values for those curves.

The TDS does not publish shelf life, density in g/cm3 beyond specific gravity, dielectric breakdown, service-temperature limits, cure depth, exact cure-through rate for TB1530, or chemical resistance beyond 10% KOH and 10% sulfuric acid immersion-change percentages.
entries.referenceSampleType: 150 g laminated tube or 460 g cartridge
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: White high-viscosity paste; 100 Pa.s; specific gravity 1.39
entries.referenceForJoining: Sealing, casting, potting and flexible bonding of metal, plastic, wood, rubber, fiber and inorganic materials, including electronic applications where low cyclic siloxane content is desirable

entries.name: TB1630

entries.cureDetail: Cut sheet to size, peel separator, transfer to the adherend by heat and pressure, laminate to the second adherend, then cure with ultraviolet irradiation. Published cure condition is cumulative light amount 30 kJ/m2 at 365 nm using a UVC-2539 metal-halide lamp.
entries.curingLightSource: UVC-2539 (Ushio Inc.) metal-halide lamp, 160 W/cm2 as printed in the TDS
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Glass/glass tensile shear strength by 3TS-301-13 after transfer, lamination and UV cure at 30 kJ/m2 / 365 nm: 5.1 MPa.
entries.peelStrength: T-peel by 3TS-304-21 after standard fabrication and UV cure: easy-bonding PET/easy-bonding PET 0.56 kN/m; no-treatment PET/no-treatment PET 0.23 kN/m.

The TDS describes measured examples, not guaranteed values.
The product must be stored in a cool dark place at -5 to 10 C and returned to room temperature before opening to prevent condensation.

The TDS does not publish tensile strength, elongation, dielectric breakdown, surface resistivity, thermal conductivity, CTE, shelf life, continuous service-temperature range, chemical resistance or pressure-sensitive open time.

entries.name: TB2202
entries.summary: Black low-temperature-curing one-part epoxy resin with trigger-cure capability for general bonding plus potting and sealing of electric parts, curing at 60 C or above with strong iron-to-iron shear and high electrical insulation.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: ThreeBond Co., Ltd. TB2202 Series Technical Data, July 7, 1995, document ED030901
entries.cureDetail: Standard iron-to-iron bonding cure conditions are 60 C for 180 min, 70 C for 50 min, or 80 C for 20 min. The series has trigger-curing ability: after initial heat, it can continue curing at normal temperature of 25 C or more. Optimum cure must be checked on actual parts because heat capacity, adjacent parts and application method change the cure.

fullCureProfiles.context: standard iron-to-iron bonding cure and physical-property cure

entries.lapShearSubstrate: Iron/iron adhesive strength under shear by JIS K 6850 after 70 C x 50 min cure: 10.0 MPa / 102 kgf/cm2.

graphOnlyProperties: trigger-cure strength build after initial heat
cautions: The TDS says curing conditions may change depending on heat capacity of the substrate and peripheral parts and the method of application; determine optimum cure on actual parts.
Plastic-substrate adhesive strength varies considerably by material manufacturer; the TDS explicitly warns to give sufficient consideration to actual use.
Store refrigerated at 5-10 C and use as soon as possible after unpacking; the TDS does not publish a shelf-life duration.
The adhesive-strength-vs-temperature and trigger-cure sections are graphs only; exact curve points are not tabulated.

The TDS does not publish tensile strength, elongation, density separate from specific gravity, thermal conductivity, service-temperature range, chemical resistance or optical properties beyond black appearance.

TB2202, TB2204, TB2206 and TB2206B are not designated as hazardous materials under Japan's Fire Defense Law in this TDS, but handling precautions still require flammables care and skin/eye protection.

entries.cureDetail: Feature statement and standard use condition specify heat curing in 30 minutes at 150 C. Property tables use 150 C x 90 min with hardening-jig heat capacity +60 min; heat and chemical resistance use 150 C for 240 h after that cure.

entries.viscosityUnit: Pa.s at 25 C, shear rate 2.0 s^-1 by 3TS-2F00-007
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Al/Al lap shear 1.8 MPa after 150 C x 90 min cure; adhesion thickness 1 mm; A1050P test piece. After 150 C x 240 h heat resistance, Al/Al lap shear 2.4 MPa; after AT oil at 150 C x 240 h, 1.8 MPa.
entries.chemicalResistance: AT oil at 150 C for 240 h after 150 C x 90 min cure: hardness A27, tensile 2.1 MPa, elongation 215%, Al/Al lap shear 1.8 MPa.
cautions: Curing failure may occur if the sealant contacts or is mixed with catalytic poisons such as water, sulfur, phosphorus, nitrogen compounds or organic metal salts.
Unseal only after material returns to room temperature to prevent condensation.
Do not dilute or mix with organic solvent or other substances.
Store in a dark dry place at -5 to 10 C avoiding direct sunlight.

The TDS does not publish peel strength, thermal conductivity, shelf life or formal service-temperature range.

entries.name: TB1655D(20t)
entries.summary: White 20 micron low-halogen epoxy heat-curable sheet adhesive for glass/metal surface bonding and electrical/electronic parts, transferred by thermocompression then cured at 110 C.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: ThreeBond Co., Ltd. ThreeBond 1655D(20t) Technical Data, October 3, 2016, document ED2016094
entries.cureDetail: Transfer adhesive to the substrate by thermocompression bonding, vacuum thermocompress to the mating substrate, then cure continuously with heat. Published cure condition is 110 C for 30 min or more.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: After 110 C x30 min cure: glass/glass lap shear 4.9 MPa with material failure; iron/iron lap shear 16.8 MPa.
cautions: If the adhesive layer is heated to 25 C or more before peeling the liner, the adhesive may adhere to the release liner and become difficult to peel.
Transfer, bonding and curing conditions can vary considerably with substrate material and size.
Some gas may be generated during operation and curing; forcibly ventilate the work and curing areas.
Store dry in a refrigerator at -5 to 10 C away from direct sunlight and unseal after returning to room temperature to prevent dew condensation.
Use may cause substrate degradation; confirm adhesive effects on substrates in advance and do not use if detrimental effects are observed.

Surface preparation is explicit: clean dirt, grease, oil, paint or loose debris and roughen with file/coarse sandpaper.
Typical values only; validate for structural, pressure, fuel-immersion or safety-critical repairs.
entries.referenceSampleType: Dark-gray 1:1 two-part epoxy in 2 oz twin tubes
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: 1:1 mix; 4-6 h set; 15-24 h cure; 6220 PSI tensile strength; 550 F maximum temperature when fully cured
entries.referenceForJoining: Strong repair bonding and filling on metal, wood, plastic/composite/PVC, ceramic, concrete, brick and fiberglass where a dark-gray machinable epoxy is acceptable

entries.name: Gorilla Glue 2-Part Epoxy
entries.summary: Clear two-part syringe epoxy with 5 min set time, gap-filling behavior, water resistance for moderate exposure, solvent resistance after cure, and official service-temperature range of -10 to 180 F.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Gorilla official product page, fetched April 24, 2026; page exposes technical specifications and SDS link rather than a separate public TDS PDF
entries.cureDetail: Dispense equal amounts from the dual syringe, mix for about 20 s until uniform, apply within 5 min while the mixture remains workable, then clamp and leave undisturbed for maximum bond strength.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Official product page publishes use and temperature properties, not lap-shear strength.
entries.moistureResistant: Water resistant; not recommended for continual water exposure
cautions: Not recommended for polyethylene, polypropylene or similar plastics.
Water resistant only for moderate exposure; the official page does not recommend continual water exposure.
Apply within 5 min after mixing because the mixture thickens and bond strength decreases with delay.
Official source is a product page with technical specifications, not a separate TDS PDF.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Mix about 20 s; 5 min set; application 50-100 F; service -10 to 180 F; cured color clear; gap filling yes
entries.referenceForJoining: Clear household and automotive repair bonding on metal, wood, ceramic, glass and compatible plastics where moderate water resistance and gap filling are useful

entries.name: Gorilla Glue Construction Adhesive
entries.summary: Gorilla Heavy Duty Construction Adhesive is a white all-weather construction adhesive with fast grab in 30 s, 24 h cure, waterproof moisture resistance, gap filling up to 1 in and service range of -40 to 200 F.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Gorilla official product page, fetched April 24, 2026; page exposes technical specifications and SDS link rather than a separate public TDS PDF
entries.cureDetail: Apply to one surface in a zig-zag or perimeter bead, press surfaces together within 10 min and hold for 20-30 s. Heavy items require temporary support; allow 24 h to cure, with slower cure at lower temperature, humidity or on nonporous surfaces.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Official product page publishes construction-adhesive standards and use properties, not lap-shear strength.
cautions: Not recommended for polyethylene, polypropylene, bitumen or similar materials.
Use mechanical fasteners with large mirrors and temporary support for heavy items.
Gap filling is stated for gaps not exceeding 1 in.
Official source is a product page with technical specifications, not a separate TDS PDF.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Application 40-100 F, best above 50 F; service -40 to 200 F; fast grab 30 s; 24 h cure; gap filling to 1 in; waterproof
entries.referenceForJoining: Indoor/outdoor construction bonding on subfloor, trim, drywall, landscaping materials, foamboard, marble and mechanically supported mirror installations

entries.name: Gorilla Glue Original (polyurethane)
entries.summary: Water-activated polyurethane Gorilla Glue that expands about 3x into materials, clamps for 1-2 h, cures best after 24 h, is waterproof to ANSI/HPVA Type I and has official service range of -40 to 200 F.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Gorilla official product page, fetched April 24, 2026; page exposes technical specifications and SDS link rather than a separate public TDS PDF
entries.cureDetail: Prepare clean, dust-free tight-fitting surfaces, roughen shiny surfaces, lightly dampen one surface with water, apply glue to the dry surface, clamp 1-2 h and allow 24 h for best cure.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Official product page publishes use and standards properties, not lap-shear strength.
cautions: Moisture activation is required; lightly dampen one surface but do not saturate.
Clamp pressure must hold surfaces together without being so high that it squeezes adhesive out of the joint.
The official technical specs say gap filling: no, despite expansion during cure.
Wear gloves and use mechanical removal after cure; uncured squeeze-out may be wiped with dry cloth or standard paint thinner.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Application above 40 F; service -40 to 200 F; clamp 1-2 h; best cure 24 h; cured color tan; ANSI/HPVA Type I; gap filling no
entries.referenceForJoining: Multi-material waterproof bonding of wood, stone, metal, ceramic, foam, glass, concrete and compatible plastics where tan expanding polyurethane squeeze-out can be managed

entries.name: Gorilla Glue Super Glue
entries.summary: Impact-tough cyanoacrylate super glue that bonds in 10-45 s without clamping, cures clear, is sandable, has no gap filling and lists official service range of -65 to 220 F.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Gorilla official product page, fetched April 24, 2026; page exposes technical specifications and SDS link rather than a separate public TDS PDF
entries.cureDetail: Apply a small amount to one clean, dry surface; one drop covers about one square inch. Press surfaces together for 10-45 s to achieve bond. Excess glue, gaps or uneven surfaces can delay or prevent bonding.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Official product page publishes service range and use properties, not lap-shear strength.
cautions: Not recommended for polyethylene, polypropylene or similar plastics.
Official technical specs list outdoor use: no.
High temperature and humidity can cause premature aging; the page says not to refrigerate.
Excess glue, gaps or uneven surfaces may delay or prevent bonding.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Room-temperature application; service -65 to 220 F; press 10-45 s; cured color clear; gap filling no; outdoor no; paintable no
entries.referenceForJoining: Fast indoor small-repair bonding on wood, metal, ceramic, rubber, leather, paper, stone and compatible plastics where minimal gap and clear CA bond line are acceptable

entries.name: IC-Gel
entries.summary: Extremely thick cyanoacrylate paste in an applicator tube, intended for non-running vertical application, void filling, plastic model putty work and aquarium coral-frag bonding, with 10-25 s set time and over-three-year shelf life stated by BSI.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: BSI official IC-Gel product page and official SDS link, fetched April 24, 2026; no separate public TDS/PDS was exposed by bsi-inc.com
entries.cureDetail: Screw top and cap unit on to puncture seal, remove cap, apply IC-Gel to one surface, hold parts tightly together, and replace cap immediately after use. BSI states set time of 10-25 s and storage in a cool dry place.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: BSI official product page publishes set time, use cases and substrate guidance but not lap-shear strength.
cautions: BSI does not publish a separate TDS/PDS or lap-shear table for IC-Gel on the official site.

BSI does not publish service-temperature limits, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, hardness, tensile strength, viscosity value, density, exact numeric gap-fill capacity or full-cure time for IC-Gel in the cached official text.
Gel continues to come out of the tube for about one second after pressure is released, so dosage can overshoot.
Sanding/file time under 20 s is stated when IC-Gel is used with BSI Insta-Set accelerator.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Paste/gel in applicator tube; sets 10-25 s; can be sanded or filed in less than 20 s with Insta-Set; shelf life over three years; BSI flags water-resistant and reinforcing uses
entries.referenceForJoining: Vertical CA bonding, model void filling/putty work, metal/plastic/fiberglass/wood/rubber hobby repairs, and aquarium coral-frag bonding where a non-running CA paste is needed

entries.name: Super-Gold
entries.summary: Odorless foam-safe cyanoacrylate family covering Super-Gold thin and Super-Gold+ medium/gap-filling grades, with non-frosting, non-fogging behavior, white-foam compatibility, and flexible cured consistency for shock absorption.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: BSI official Super-Gold product page and official SDS link, fetched April 24, 2026; no separate public TDS/PDS was exposed by bsi-inc.com
entries.cureDetail: BSI directions tab was not populated. The product page states Super-Gold thin and Super-Gold+ gap-filling medium take only 2 or 3 s longer to bond than Insta-Cure/Insta-Cure+, can bond wood to white foam with Super-Gold+ in less than 15 s, and can be accelerated for foam-to-foam with a very light fog of Insta-Set.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: BSI official product page publishes foam-safe/odorless behavior and application examples but not lap-shear strength.
cautions: BSI does not publish a separate TDS/PDS or lap-shear table for Super-Gold on the official site.

BSI does not publish service-temperature limits, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, hardness, tensile strength, viscosity value, density, exact numeric gap-fill capacity, general fixture time, full-cure time or shelf life for Super-Gold in the cached official text.
For foam-to-foam bonding BSI warns that excess Insta-Set may create too much heat and melt foam.
BSI says Maxi-Cure is still recommended for most of a plastic kit, with Super-Gold+ ideal for attaching clear canopies.
entries.referenceSampleType: Super-Gold thin and Super-Gold+ medium/gap-filling odorless foam-safe CA
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Odorless, non-frosting, foam safe, clear-plastic non-fogging CA family; Super-Gold+ bonds wood to white foam in less than 15 s; both cure more flexible than standard CA
entries.referenceForJoining: White foam, rubber, clear plastic canopies, balsa/wood, fiberglass, Kevlar saturation and visible hobby assemblies where standard CA odor, foam attack or blooming is unacceptable

entries.name: Quik-Cure 5 Minute Epoxy
entries.summary: Equal two-part 5 min hobby epoxy that cures slightly flexible and non-brittle, can be handled after 15 min, reaches full strength in 1 h, and cures down to 40 F while resisting most chemicals after cure.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: BSI official Quik-Cure product page and official SDS link, fetched April 24, 2026; no separate public TDS/PDS was exposed by bsi-inc.com
entries.cureDetail: Equal two-part resin/hardener hobby adhesive. BSI states it forms high-strength bonds through chemical reaction, cures to a slightly flexible consistency, can be mixed with microballoons as a quick-setting putty, and bonds best to clean textured surfaces; smooth non-porous surfaces should be roughened.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: BSI official product page publishes handling/full-strength timing and substrate guidance but not lap-shear strength.
cautions: Not recommended for areas subject to long-term immersion in water.
Temperatures above 90 F shorten the working time from 5 min to less than 4 min.
Smooth non-porous surfaces should be roughened with coarse sandpaper for better adhesion.
BSI does not publish a separate TDS/PDS or lap-shear table for Quik-Cure on the official site.
entries.referenceSampleType: Equal two-part 5 min slightly flexible epoxy
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: 5 min working time at normal conditions; less than 4 min above 90 F; cures down to 40 F; handle after 15 min; full strength in 1 h; full cure stated as less than 15 min
entries.referenceForJoining: Fast hobby bonding of hard wood, glass, metal, balsa, soft wood, fiberglass, ceramics, hard plastics and reinforced assemblies where a slightly flexible non-brittle epoxy is useful

entries.name: 3.0 Advanced Sealant
entries.summary: Advanced hybrid-polymer window, door, siding and trim sealant with wet/damp surface adhesion, 20-120 F application range, 30 min rain/paint/water ready time, 24 h full cure, ASTM C920 Class 35 rating and 100% waterproof/weatherproof performance.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: DAP 3.0 High Performance Window, Door, Siding & Trim Sealant TDS, July 24, 2019
entries.cureDetail: Apply to clean, dry, structurally sound joints, tool before skin-over, allow at least 30 min before water exposure, and allow 24 h full cure. Paintable in 30 min with high-quality acrylic latex coatings; use acrylic primer for oil-based paints.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: DAP TDS publishes sealant movement/service properties rather than lap-shear strength.
cautions: Not recommended for continuous underwater use, high-temperature surfaces or surface defects.
Joint width should not exceed 1/2 in; if joint depth exceeds 1/2 in use foam backer rod.
Certain porous substrates such as concrete may require primer for best adhesion.
Store below 80 F in a dry place for optimal shelf life.
entries.referenceCategory: Hybrid Window, Door, Siding and Trim Sealants
entries.referenceSampleType: White advanced hybrid-polymer gunnable non-sag sealant
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Advanced hybrid polymer; >99% solids; density 12.2 lb/gal; 20 min tooling; 2 h tack-free; 30 min water/paint/return-to-service; 24 h full cure; service -65 to 190 F continuous
entries.referenceForJoining: Interior/exterior sealing of windows, doors, siding, trim, baseboards, molding, gutters, flashing, pipes, vents, ducts, butt joints, corner joints and above-ground foundations

entries.name: Dynaflex 230
entries.summary: Premium elastomeric acrylic latex sealant with silicone-like toughness for windows, doors, siding, trim and baseboards, with ASTM C920 Class 25 movement, 100% waterproof/weatherproof seal, low odor, water cleanup and white/colors vs clear variant cure timing preserved separately.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: DAP DYNAFLEX 230 Premium Elastomeric Sealant TDS for white/colors and clear variants, February 25, 2019; official product page fetched April 24, 2026
entries.cureDetail: Apply above 40 F to clean, dry, structurally sound joints. Tool before sealant skins over, clean wet sealant with water, allow white/colors to dry 2-4 h before painting and clear to dry clear for about 7-14 days before painting.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: DAP TDS publishes sealant movement/service properties rather than lap-shear strength.

clear.notes: Clear applies white and dries clear in 7-14 days depending on joint depth, temperature and humidity.

cautions: Do not apply when rain or freezing temperatures are forecasted within 24 h; cooler temperatures and higher humidity slow dry time.
Not for continuous underwater use or filling surface defects.
Joint size should not exceed 2 in wide by 1/2 in deep; use backer rod if depth exceeds 1/2 in.
Clear variant dries clear and becomes paint-ready in 7-14 days, much slower than white/colors.
entries.referenceCategory: Acrylic Latex Elastomeric Window, Door, Siding and Trim Sealants
entries.referenceSampleType: Gunnable non-sag advanced acrylic polymer latex sealant, white/colors and clear variants
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: White/colors: 78% solids, 12 lb/gal, 10 min tooling, 30 min tack-free, 24 h dry through, 2 h paint ready. Clear: 59% solids, 8.6 lb/gal, 60 min tooling/tack-free, 7-14 d dry-clear/paint-ready.
entries.referenceForJoining: Air-leak and weather sealing around windows, doors, siding, trim, molding, pipes, vents, corner joints, butt joints, tuck pointing and other exterior/interior gaps where paintability and movement tolerance matter

entries.name: Oatey Clear PVC Medium-Bodied / PVC Plastic-Weld 404 Series
entries.summary: Medium-bodied clear PVC solvent cement for all schedules and classes of PVC pipe and fittings up to 6 in with interference fit, with 40-110 F use range, 1-6 min set-time table, 15 min handling strength and 30 s hold time.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: Oatey Plasti-Weld 404 Series Medium Clear PVC Solvent Cement Technical Specification LCS1733, September 2022; official product page fetched April 24, 2026
entries.chemistry: PVC solvent cement with PVC resin and solvent carrier
entries.cureDetail: Use on clean, square-cut, deburred and chamfered PVC pipe/fittings. Prime with a listed primer, apply cement while primer is wet, assemble quickly with a quarter-turn, hold 30 s to prevent push-out, allow 15 min handling strength, and allow full cure before testing.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: PVC lap-shear strength from Oatey Plasti-Weld 404 submittal; 72 h value 900 psi converted to 6.21 MPa.
cautions: Not for systems using or being tested by compressed air or gases.
At temperatures outside 40-110 F, special care is required to make good joints.
Do not thin; do not use if gelled.
Puddling inside fittings can weaken PVC pipe or fittings.
Flammable solvent cement; use ventilation and PPE per official submittal/SDS.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Clear liquid; minimum 500 cps at 73 F +/- 2 F; 7.78 +/- 0.2 lb/gal density; 425 g/L VOC; set 1-6 min depending on temperature; 3 year shelf life
entries.referenceForJoining: Solvent welding all schedules and classes of PVC pipe and fittings up to 6 in with interference fit for potable water, pressure pipe, sewer, drain, waste, vent and conduit systems
entries.serviceTemperatureNote: Application temperature 4–43°C (40–110°F); cured joint service temperature not explicitly stated in TDS; serviceMax 60°C is the ASTM D2564 test temperature; minimum for cured joint not stated.

entries.name: Weld-On 4007
entries.summary: Clear medium-bodied low-VOC high-strength solvent cement for rigid and foamed PVC, with 20 min working time, 25 min fixture time, 72 h ultimate bond strength and published PVC/ABS bond-strength table.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: IPS Adhesives Weld-On 4007 Technical Data Sheet, January 2020; current official Weld-On site did not expose this legacy industrial PDS directly, so an IPS-authored distributor mirror was used
entries.chemistry: PVC solvent cement for rigid and foamed PVC
entries.cureDetail: Apply by brush, spray, roller coating, pump-type oil can, polyethylene squeeze bottle or similar device to one or both clean smooth surfaces. Assemble while wet and apply firm contact; 2-10 psi pressure can materially increase bond strength.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: PVC aged bond strength at 24 h from IPS Weld-On 4007 TDS, 500 psi converted to 3.45 MPa; one-week PVC value is 900 psi.
cautions: Use only with adequate ventilation; product is flammable and solvent vapors are hazardous.
Some molded and extruded parts may require special priming; verify by test.
Clamp/jig pressure time varies: a few minutes for well-fitting modest-load parts, a few hours or more for difficult joints.
Official current Weld-On product-data page did not expose a direct 4007 page/PDF; this record uses an IPS-authored TDS mirror.
entries.referenceCategory: Rigid and Foamed PVC Fabrication Solvent Cements
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: 800 +/- 200 cps; SG 0.934 +/- 0.01; working time 20 min; fixture time 25 min; ultimate bond strength 72 h; 3 year shelf life
entries.referenceForJoining: Rigid PVC and foamed PVC display, sign, lighting, houseware, container, toy, electrical/electronic assembly and decorator-item fabrication

entries.name: Weld-On 3 DCM TCE FREE
entries.summary: Water-thin DCM/TCE-free acrylic solvent cement with 1 min working time, 2 min fixture time, 24 h to 65-80% ultimate bond strength and 2825 psi acrylic bond strength at 24 h.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: IPS Adhesives Weld-On 3 DCM TCE Free Technical Data Sheet, copyright 2025; IPS-authored distributor mirror used because the current Weld-On product-data page does not expose this acrylic fabrication TDS directly
entries.cureDetail: Apply with syringe, eyedropper or brush to clean dry intimate-fit parts and assemble while wet. Capillary method flows approximately 0.25 in between lightly placed surfaces; soak method softens surfaces for about 2-5 min before joining.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Acrylic aged bond strength at 24 h from IPS Weld-On 3 DCM TCE Free TDS, 2825 psi converted to 19.48 MPa.
cautions: Industrial and professional use only; not for consumer use.
Not recommended for cross-linked acrylic.
High humidity can cause blushing; Weld-On 4 DCM TCE FREE is recommended where more blush resistance is needed.
In many cases it dries too fast to brush onto parts before joining.
Adding thinners to change viscosity is not recommended.
entries.referenceSampleType: Clear water-thin DCM/TCE-free acrylic solvent cement
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Water thin; 1 min working time; 2 min fixture time; SG 1.218 +/- 0.01; 732/2825/2924 psi acrylic strength at 2 h/24 h/1 week
entries.referenceForJoining: Capillary and soak-method bonding of cast and extruded acrylic for signs, POP displays, containers, housewares, bottles and other acrylic fabrication
entries.serviceTemperatureNote: No service temperature range stated in TDS.

entries.name: Weld-On 16 DCM TCE FREE
entries.summary: Clear medium-bodied DCM/TCE-free acrylic solvent cement with 800 +/- 100 cps viscosity, 5 min working time, 10 min fixture time and 2626 psi acrylic bond strength at 24 h.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: IPS Adhesives Weld-On 16 DCM TCE Free Technical Data Sheet, copyright 2025; IPS-authored distributor mirror used because the current Weld-On product-data page does not expose this acrylic fabrication TDS directly
entries.cureDetail: Apply to one or both clean intimate-fit surfaces with brush, polyethylene squeeze bottle or gun. If applied to one surface, bring surfaces into gentle contact for several seconds to soften the dry surface, then assemble with firm pressure while wet and hold or clamp until initial set.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Acrylic aged bond strength at 24 h from IPS Weld-On 16 DCM TCE Free TDS, 2626 psi converted to 18.11 MPa.
cautions: Industrial and professional use only; not for consumer use.
Can be thinned with Weld-On 3 DCM TCE FREE by approximately 10%, but changing viscosity can change properties.
Flammable fast-evaporating solvent cement; follow SDS ventilation/PPE requirements.
Hold or clamp until initial set; strength continues to increase for weeks.
entries.referenceSampleType: Clear medium-bodied DCM/TCE-free acrylic solvent cement
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: 800 +/- 100 cps; 5 min working time; 10 min fixture time; SG 1.202 +/- 0.01; 761/2626/3091 psi acrylic strength at 2 h/24 h/1 week
entries.referenceForJoining: General-purpose high-strength bonding of acrylic signs, plastic letters, industrial fabrications, display items, lenses and models, with use also on butyrate, ABS and polycarbonate
entries.serviceTemperatureNote: No service temperature range stated in TDS.

entries.name: Weld-On 40
entries.summary: Two-component clear low-VOC reactive acrylic adhesive for acrylic and mixed plastics, with 20 min working time, 2 h fixture time, 72 h ultimate strength and 2600 psi room-temperature 24 h bond strength.
entries.sourceRevisionDate: IPS Adhesives Weld-On 40 Technical Data Sheet, January 2020; IPS-authored distributor mirror used because the current Weld-On product-data page does not expose this acrylic fabrication TDS directly
entries.cureDetail: Bring components to room temperature, optionally adjust Component A with no more than 10% Weld-On 3061, then add 5 cc or 5 g Component B per 100 cc or 100 g Component A and mix completely. Apply to one or both surfaces and assemble immediately with just enough pressure to remove air bubbles.
entries.lapShearSubstrate: Room-temperature aged bond strength at 24 h from IPS Weld-On 40 TDS, 2600 psi converted to 17.93 MPa.
entries.mixRatio: 100 parts Component A to 5 parts Component B by volume or weight
cautions: Sand surfaces with 240-400 grit before bonding and do not flame-polish bonding surfaces.
Anneal acrylic after machining/forming to reduce crazing risk.
Do not squeeze parts so hard that adhesive is forced out, causing a dry joint.
Pot life shortens with larger masses or temperatures above 75 F.
Store sealed away from direct sunlight in a cool 50-80 F dry place.
entries.referenceSampleType: Clear two-component medium-syrupy low-VOC reactive acrylic adhesive
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: 2900 +/- 150 cps; 20 min working time; 2 h fixture; tack-free 7-8 min at 0.020 in; handle 4 h; machine after 24 h; ultimate strength 72 h
entries.referenceForJoining: High-clarity bonding of extruded, cast and cross-linked acrylic to itself and to polyester, cellulose acetate butyrate, polycarbonate, PVC, ABS and PETG in signs, aquariums, museum displays and plastic fabrication
